2342				foreach(arg, xexpr->named_args)
2343				{
2344					Expr	   *e = (Expr *) lfirst(arg);
2345
2346					ExecInitExprRec(e, state,
2347									&scratch.d.xmlexpr.named_argvalue[off],
2348									&scratch.d.xmlexpr.named_argnull[off]);
2349					off++;
2350				}
2351
2352				off = 0;
2353				foreach(arg, xexpr->args)
2354				{
2355					Expr	   *e = (Expr *) lfirst(arg);
2356
2357					ExecInitExprRec(e, state,
2358									&scratch.d.xmlexpr.argvalue[off],
2359									&scratch.d.xmlexpr.argnull[off]);
2360					off++;
2361				}
2362
2363				/* and evaluate the actual XML expression */
2364				ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2365				break;
2366			}
2367
2368		case T_JsonValueExpr:
2369			{
2370				JsonValueExpr *jve = (JsonValueExpr *) node;
2371
2372				Assert(jve->raw_expr != NULL);
2373				ExecInitExprRec(jve->raw_expr, state, resv, resnull);
2374				Assert(jve->formatted_expr != NULL);
2375				ExecInitExprRec(jve->formatted_expr, state, resv, resnull);
2376				break;
2377			}
2378
2379		case T_JsonConstructorExpr:
2380			{
2381				JsonConstructorExpr *ctor = (JsonConstructorExpr *) node;
2382				List	   *args = ctor->args;
2383				ListCell   *lc;
2384				int			nargs = list_length(args);
2385				int			argno = 0;
2386
2387				if (ctor->func)
2388				{
2389					ExecInitExprRec(ctor->func, state, resv, resnull);
2390				}
2391				else if ((ctor->type == JSCTOR_JSON_PARSE && !ctor->unique) ||
2392						 ctor->type == JSCTOR_JSON_SERIALIZE)
2393				{
2394					/* Use the value of the first argument as result */
2395					ExecInitExprRec(linitial(args), state, resv, resnull);
2396				}
2397				else
2398				{
2399					JsonConstructorExprState *jcstate;
2400
2401					jcstate = palloc0(sizeof(JsonConstructorExprState));
2402
2403					scratch.opcode = EEOP_JSON_CONSTRUCTOR;
2404					scratch.d.json_constructor.jcstate = jcstate;
2405
2406					jcstate->constructor = ctor;
2407					jcstate->arg_values = (Datum *) palloc(sizeof(Datum) * nargs);
2408					jcstate->arg_nulls = (bool *) palloc(sizeof(bool) * nargs);
2409					jcstate->arg_types = (Oid *) palloc(sizeof(Oid) * nargs);
2410					jcstate->nargs = nargs;
2411
2412					foreach(lc, args)
2413					{
2414						Expr	   *arg = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
2415
2416						jcstate->arg_types[argno] = exprType((Node *) arg);
2417
2418						if (IsA(arg, Const))
2419						{
2420							/* Don't evaluate const arguments every round */
2421							Const	   *con = (Const *) arg;
2422
2423							jcstate->arg_values[argno] = con->constvalue;
2424							jcstate->arg_nulls[argno] = con->constisnull;
2425						}
2426						else
2427						{
2428							ExecInitExprRec(arg, state,
2429											&jcstate->arg_values[argno],
2430											&jcstate->arg_nulls[argno]);
2431						}
2432						argno++;
2433					}
2434
2435					/* prepare type cache for datum_to_json[b]() */
2436					if (ctor->type == JSCTOR_JSON_SCALAR)
2437					{
2438						bool		is_jsonb =
2439							ctor->returning->format->format_type == JS_FORMAT_JSONB;
2440
2441						jcstate->arg_type_cache =
2442							palloc(sizeof(*jcstate->arg_type_cache) * nargs);
2443
2444						for (int i = 0; i < nargs; i++)
2445						{
2446							JsonTypeCategory category;
2447							Oid			outfuncid;
2448							Oid			typid = jcstate->arg_types[i];
2449
2450							json_categorize_type(typid, is_jsonb,
2451												 &category, &outfuncid);
2452
2453							jcstate->arg_type_cache[i].outfuncid = outfuncid;
2454							jcstate->arg_type_cache[i].category = (int) category;
2455						}
2456					}
2457
2458					ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2459				}
2460
2461				if (ctor->coercion)
2462				{
2463					Datum	   *innermost_caseval = state->innermost_caseval;
2464					bool	   *innermost_isnull = state->innermost_casenull;
2465
2466					state->innermost_caseval = resv;
2467					state->innermost_casenull = resnull;
2468
2469					ExecInitExprRec(ctor->coercion, state, resv, resnull);
2470
2471					state->innermost_caseval = innermost_caseval;
2472					state->innermost_casenull = innermost_isnull;
2473				}
2474			}
2475			break;
2476
2477		case T_JsonIsPredicate:
2478			{
2479				JsonIsPredicate *pred = (JsonIsPredicate *) node;
2480
2481				ExecInitExprRec((Expr *) pred->expr, state, resv, resnull);
2482
2483				scratch.opcode = EEOP_IS_JSON;
2484				scratch.d.is_json.pred = pred;
2485
2486				ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2487				break;
2488			}
2489
2490		case T_JsonExpr:
2491			{
2492				JsonExpr   *jsexpr = castNode(JsonExpr, node);
2493
2494				/*
2495				 * No need to initialize a full JsonExprState For
2496				 * JSON_TABLE(), because the upstream caller tfuncFetchRows()
2497				 * is only interested in the value of formatted_expr.
2498				 */
2499				if (jsexpr->op == JSON_TABLE_OP)
2500					ExecInitExprRec((Expr *) jsexpr->formatted_expr, state,
2501									resv, resnull);
2502				else
2503					ExecInitJsonExpr(jsexpr, state, resv, resnull, &scratch);
2504				break;
2505			}
2506
2507		case T_NullTest:
2508			{
2509				NullTest   *ntest = (NullTest *) node;
2510
2511				if (ntest->nulltesttype == IS_NULL)
2512				{
2513					if (ntest->argisrow)
2514						scratch.opcode = EEOP_NULLTEST_ROWISNULL;
2515					else
2516						scratch.opcode = EEOP_NULLTEST_ISNULL;
2517				}
2518				else if (ntest->nulltesttype == IS_NOT_NULL)
2519				{
2520					if (ntest->argisrow)
2521						scratch.opcode = EEOP_NULLTEST_ROWISNOTNULL;
2522					else
2523						scratch.opcode = EEOP_NULLTEST_ISNOTNULL;
2524				}
2525				else
2526				{
2527					elog(ERROR, "unrecognized nulltesttype: %d",
2528						 (int) ntest->nulltesttype);
2529				}
2530				/* initialize cache in case it's a row test */
2531				scratch.d.nulltest_row.rowcache.cacheptr = NULL;
2532
2533				/* first evaluate argument into result variable */
2534				ExecInitExprRec(ntest->arg, state,
2535								resv, resnull);
2536
2537				/* then push the test of that argument */
2538				ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2539				break;
2540			}
2541
2542		case T_BooleanTest:
2543			{
2544				BooleanTest *btest = (BooleanTest *) node;
2545
2546				/*
2547				 * Evaluate argument, directly into result datum.  That's ok,
2548				 * because resv/resnull is definitely not used anywhere else,
2549				 * and will get overwritten by the below EEOP_BOOLTEST_IS_*
2550				 * step.
2551				 */
2552				ExecInitExprRec(btest->arg, state, resv, resnull);
2553
2554				switch (btest->booltesttype)
2555				{
2556					case IS_TRUE:
2557						scratch.opcode = EEOP_BOOLTEST_IS_TRUE;
2558						break;
2559					case IS_NOT_TRUE:
2560						scratch.opcode = EEOP_BOOLTEST_IS_NOT_TRUE;
2561						break;
2562					case IS_FALSE:
2563						scratch.opcode = EEOP_BOOLTEST_IS_FALSE;
2564						break;
2565					case IS_NOT_FALSE:
2566						scratch.opcode = EEOP_BOOLTEST_IS_NOT_FALSE;
2567						break;
2568					case IS_UNKNOWN:
2569						/* Same as scalar IS NULL test */
2570						scratch.opcode = EEOP_NULLTEST_ISNULL;
2571						break;
2572					case IS_NOT_UNKNOWN:
2573						/* Same as scalar IS NOT NULL test */
2574						scratch.opcode = EEOP_NULLTEST_ISNOTNULL;
2575						break;
2576					default:
2577						elog(ERROR, "unrecognized booltesttype: %d",
2578							 (int) btest->booltesttype);
2579				}
2580
2581				ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2582				break;
2583			}
2584
2585		case T_CoerceToDomain:
2586			{
2587				CoerceToDomain *ctest = (CoerceToDomain *) node;
2588
2589				ExecInitCoerceToDomain(&scratch, ctest, state,
2590									   resv, resnull);
2591				break;
2592			}
2593
2594		case T_CoerceToDomainValue:
2595			{
2596				/*
2597				 * Read from location identified by innermost_domainval.  Note
2598				 * that innermost_domainval could be NULL, if we're compiling
2599				 * a standalone domain check rather than one embedded in a
2600				 * larger expression.  In that case we must read from
2601				 * econtext->domainValue_datum.  We'll take care of that by
2602				 * generating a specialized operation.
2603				 */
2604				if (state->innermost_domainval == NULL)
2605					scratch.opcode = EEOP_DOMAIN_TESTVAL_EXT;
2606				else
2607				{
2608					scratch.opcode = EEOP_DOMAIN_TESTVAL;
2609					/* we share instruction union variant with case testval */
2610					scratch.d.casetest.value = state->innermost_domainval;
2611					scratch.d.casetest.isnull = state->innermost_domainnull;
2612				}
2613				ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2614				break;
2615			}
2616
2617		case T_CurrentOfExpr:
2618			{
2619				scratch.opcode = EEOP_CURRENTOFEXPR;
2620				ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2621				break;
2622			}
2623
2624		case T_NextValueExpr:
2625			{
2626				NextValueExpr *nve = (NextValueExpr *) node;
2627
2628				scratch.opcode = EEOP_NEXTVALUEEXPR;
2629				scratch.d.nextvalueexpr.seqid = nve->seqid;
2630				scratch.d.nextvalueexpr.seqtypid = nve->typeId;
2631
2632				ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2633				break;
2634			}
2635
2636		case T_ReturningExpr:
2637			{
2638				ReturningExpr *rexpr = (ReturningExpr *) node;
2639				int			retstep;
2640
2641				/* Skip expression evaluation if OLD/NEW row doesn't exist */
2642				scratch.opcode = EEOP_RETURNINGEXPR;
2643				scratch.d.returningexpr.nullflag = rexpr->retold ?
2644					EEO_FLAG_OLD_IS_NULL : EEO_FLAG_NEW_IS_NULL;
2645				scratch.d.returningexpr.jumpdone = -1;	/* set below */
2646				ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2647				retstep = state->steps_len - 1;
2648
2649				/* Steps to evaluate expression to return */
2650				ExecInitExprRec(rexpr->retexpr, state, resv, resnull);
2651
2652				/* Jump target used if OLD/NEW row doesn't exist */
2653				state->steps[retstep].d.returningexpr.jumpdone = state->steps_len;
2654
2655				/* Update ExprState flags */
2656				if (rexpr->retold)
2657					state->flags |= EEO_FLAG_HAS_OLD;
2658				else
2659					state->flags |= EEO_FLAG_HAS_NEW;
2660
2661				break;
2662			}
2663
2664		default:
2665			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d",
2666				 (int) nodeTag(node));
2667			break;
2668	}
2669}
2670
2671/*
2672 * Add another expression evaluation step to ExprState->steps.
2673 *
2674 * Note that this potentially re-allocates es->steps, therefore no pointer
2675 * into that array may be used while the expression is still being built.
2676 */
2677void
2678ExprEvalPushStep(ExprState *es, const ExprEvalStep *s)
2679{
2680	if (es->steps_alloc == 0)
2681	{
2682		es->steps_alloc = 16;
2683		es->steps = palloc(sizeof(ExprEvalStep) * es->steps_alloc);
2684	}
2685	else if (es->steps_alloc == es->steps_len)
2686	{
2687		es->steps_alloc *= 2;
2688		es->steps = repalloc(es->steps,
2689							 sizeof(ExprEvalStep) * es->steps_alloc);
2690	}
2691
2692	memcpy(&es->steps[es->steps_len++], s, sizeof(ExprEvalStep));
2693}
2694
2695/*
2696 * Perform setup necessary for the evaluation of a function-like expression,
2697 * appending argument evaluation steps to the steps list in *state, and
2698 * setting up *scratch so it is ready to be pushed.
2699 *
2700 * *scratch is not pushed here, so that callers may override the opcode,
2701 * which is useful for function-like cases like DISTINCT.
2702 */
2703static void
2704ExecInitFunc(ExprEvalStep *scratch, Expr *node, List *args, Oid funcid,
2705			 Oid inputcollid, ExprState *state)
2706{
2707	int			nargs = list_length(args);
2708	AclResult	aclresult;
2709	FmgrInfo   *flinfo;
2710	FunctionCallInfo fcinfo;
2711	int			argno;
2712	ListCell   *lc;
2713
2714	/* Check permission to call function */
2715	aclresult = object_aclcheck(ProcedureRelationId, funcid, GetUserId(), ACL_EXECUTE);
2716	if (aclresult != ACLCHECK_OK)
2717		aclcheck_error(aclresult, OBJECT_FUNCTION, get_func_name(funcid));
2718	InvokeFunctionExecuteHook(funcid);
2719
2720	/*
2721	 * Safety check on nargs.  Under normal circumstances this should never
2722	 * fail, as parser should check sooner.  But possibly it might fail if
2723	 * server has been compiled with FUNC_MAX_ARGS smaller than some functions
2724	 * declared in pg_proc?
2725	 */
2726	if (nargs > FUNC_MAX_ARGS)
2727		ereport(ERROR,
2728				(errcode(ERRCODE_TOO_MANY_ARGUMENTS),
2729				 errmsg_plural("cannot pass more than %d argument to a function",
2730							   "cannot pass more than %d arguments to a function",
2731							   FUNC_MAX_ARGS,
2732							   FUNC_MAX_ARGS)));
2733
2734	/* Allocate function lookup data and parameter workspace for this call */
2735	scratch->d.func.finfo = palloc0(sizeof(FmgrInfo));
2736	scratch->d.func.fcinfo_data = palloc0(SizeForFunctionCallInfo(nargs));
2737	flinfo = scratch->d.func.finfo;
2738	fcinfo = scratch->d.func.fcinfo_data;
2739
2740	/* Set up the primary fmgr lookup information */
2741	fmgr_info(funcid, flinfo);
2742	fmgr_info_set_expr((Node *) node, flinfo);
2743
2744	/* Initialize function call parameter structure too */
2745	InitFunctionCallInfoData(*fcinfo, flinfo,
2746							 nargs, inputcollid, NULL, NULL);
2747
2748	/* Keep extra copies of this info to save an indirection at runtime */
2749	scratch->d.func.fn_addr = flinfo->fn_addr;
2750	scratch->d.func.nargs = nargs;
2751
2752	/* We only support non-set functions here */
2753	if (flinfo->fn_retset)
2754		ereport(ERROR,
2755				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2756				 errmsg("set-valued function called in context that cannot accept a set"),
2757				 state->parent ?
2758				 executor_errposition(state->parent->state,
2759									  exprLocation((Node *) node)) : 0));
2760
2761	/* Build code to evaluate arguments directly into the fcinfo struct */
2762	argno = 0;
2763	foreach(lc, args)
2764	{
2765		Expr	   *arg = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
2766
2767		if (IsA(arg, Const))
2768		{
2769			/*
2770			 * Don't evaluate const arguments every round; especially
2771			 * interesting for constants in comparisons.
2772			 */
2773			Const	   *con = (Const *) arg;
2774
2775			fcinfo->args[argno].value = con->constvalue;
2776			fcinfo->args[argno].isnull = con->constisnull;
2777		}
2778		else
2779		{
2780			ExecInitExprRec(arg, state,
2781							&fcinfo->args[argno].value,
2782							&fcinfo->args[argno].isnull);
2783		}
2784		argno++;
2785	}
2786
2787	/* Insert appropriate opcode depending on strictness and stats level */
2788	if (pgstat_track_functions <= flinfo->fn_stats)
2789	{
2790		if (flinfo->fn_strict && nargs > 0)
2791		{
2792			/* Choose nargs optimized implementation if available. */
2793			if (nargs == 1)
2794				scratch->opcode = EEOP_FUNCEXPR_STRICT_1;
2795			else if (nargs == 2)
2796				scratch->opcode = EEOP_FUNCEXPR_STRICT_2;
2797			else
2798				scratch->opcode = EEOP_FUNCEXPR_STRICT;
2799		}
2800		else
2801			scratch->opcode = EEOP_FUNCEXPR;
2802	}
2803	else
2804	{
2805		if (flinfo->fn_strict && nargs > 0)
2806			scratch->opcode = EEOP_FUNCEXPR_STRICT_FUSAGE;
2807		else
2808			scratch->opcode = EEOP_FUNCEXPR_FUSAGE;
2809	}
2810}
2811
2812/*
2813 * Append the steps necessary for the evaluation of a SubPlan node to
2814 * ExprState->steps.
2815 *
2816 * subplan - SubPlan expression to evaluate
2817 * state - ExprState to whose ->steps to append the necessary operations
2818 * resv / resnull - where to store the result of the node into
2819 */
2820static void
2821ExecInitSubPlanExpr(SubPlan *subplan,
2822					ExprState *state,
2823					Datum *resv, bool *resnull)
2824{
2825	ExprEvalStep scratch = {0};
2826	SubPlanState *sstate;
2827	ListCell   *pvar;
2828	ListCell   *l;
2829
2830	if (!state->parent)
2831		elog(ERROR, "SubPlan found with no parent plan");
2832
2833	/*
2834	 * Generate steps to evaluate input arguments for the subplan.
2835	 *
2836	 * We evaluate the argument expressions into resv/resnull, and then use
2837	 * PARAM_SET to update the parameter. We do that, instead of evaluating
2838	 * directly into the param, to avoid depending on the pointer value
2839	 * remaining stable / being included in the generated expression. It's ok
2840	 * to use resv/resnull for multiple params, as each parameter evaluation
2841	 * is immediately followed by an EEOP_PARAM_SET (and thus are saved before
2842	 * they could be overwritten again).
2843	 *
2844	 * Any calculation we have to do can be done in the parent econtext, since
2845	 * the Param values don't need to have per-query lifetime.
2846	 */
2847	Assert(list_length(subplan->parParam) == list_length(subplan->args));
2848	forboth(l, subplan->parParam, pvar, subplan->args)
2849	{
2850		int			paramid = lfirst_int(l);
2851		Expr	   *arg = (Expr *) lfirst(pvar);
2852
2853		ExecInitExprRec(arg, state, resv, resnull);
2854
2855		scratch.opcode = EEOP_PARAM_SET;
2856		scratch.resvalue = resv;
2857		scratch.resnull = resnull;
2858		scratch.d.param.paramid = paramid;
2859		/* paramtype's not actually used, but we might as well fill it */
2860		scratch.d.param.paramtype = exprType((Node *) arg);
2861		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2862	}
2863
2864	sstate = ExecInitSubPlan(subplan, state->parent);
2865
2866	/* add SubPlanState nodes to state->parent->subPlan */
2867	state->parent->subPlan = lappend(state->parent->subPlan,
2868									 sstate);
2869
2870	scratch.opcode = EEOP_SUBPLAN;
2871	scratch.resvalue = resv;
2872	scratch.resnull = resnull;
2873	scratch.d.subplan.sstate = sstate;
2874
2875	ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2876}
2877
2878/*
2879 * Add expression steps performing setup that's needed before any of the
2880 * main execution of the expression.
2881 */
2882static void
2883ExecCreateExprSetupSteps(ExprState *state, Node *node)
2884{
2885	ExprSetupInfo info = {0, 0, 0, 0, 0, NIL};
2886
2887	/* Prescan to find out what we need. */
2888	expr_setup_walker(node, &info);
2889
2890	/* And generate those steps. */
2891	ExecPushExprSetupSteps(state, &info);
2892}
2893
2894/*
2895 * Add steps performing expression setup as indicated by "info".
2896 * This is useful when building an ExprState covering more than one expression.
2897 */
2898static void
2899ExecPushExprSetupSteps(ExprState *state, ExprSetupInfo *info)
2900{
2901	ExprEvalStep scratch = {0};
2902	ListCell   *lc;
2903
2904	scratch.resvalue = NULL;
2905	scratch.resnull = NULL;
2906
2907	/*
2908	 * Add steps deforming the ExprState's inner/outer/scan/old/new slots as
2909	 * much as required by any Vars appearing in the expression.
2910	 */
2911	if (info->last_inner > 0)
2912	{
2913		scratch.opcode = EEOP_INNER_FETCHSOME;
2914		scratch.d.fetch.last_var = info->last_inner;
2915		scratch.d.fetch.fixed = false;
2916		scratch.d.fetch.kind = NULL;
2917		scratch.d.fetch.known_desc = NULL;
2918		if (ExecComputeSlotInfo(state, &scratch))
2919			ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2920	}
2921	if (info->last_outer > 0)
2922	{
2923		scratch.opcode = EEOP_OUTER_FETCHSOME;
2924		scratch.d.fetch.last_var = info->last_outer;
2925		scratch.d.fetch.fixed = false;
2926		scratch.d.fetch.kind = NULL;
2927		scratch.d.fetch.known_desc = NULL;
2928		if (ExecComputeSlotInfo(state, &scratch))
2929			ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2930	}
2931	if (info->last_scan > 0)
2932	{
2933		scratch.opcode = EEOP_SCAN_FETCHSOME;
2934		scratch.d.fetch.last_var = info->last_scan;
2935		scratch.d.fetch.fixed = false;
2936		scratch.d.fetch.kind = NULL;
2937		scratch.d.fetch.known_desc = NULL;
2938		if (ExecComputeSlotInfo(state, &scratch))
2939			ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2940	}
2941	if (info->last_old > 0)
2942	{
2943		scratch.opcode = EEOP_OLD_FETCHSOME;
2944		scratch.d.fetch.last_var = info->last_old;
2945		scratch.d.fetch.fixed = false;
2946		scratch.d.fetch.kind = NULL;
2947		scratch.d.fetch.known_desc = NULL;
2948		if (ExecComputeSlotInfo(state, &scratch))
2949			ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2950	}
2951	if (info->last_new > 0)
2952	{
2953		scratch.opcode = EEOP_NEW_FETCHSOME;
2954		scratch.d.fetch.last_var = info->last_new;
2955		scratch.d.fetch.fixed = false;
2956		scratch.d.fetch.kind = NULL;
2957		scratch.d.fetch.known_desc = NULL;
2958		if (ExecComputeSlotInfo(state, &scratch))
2959			ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
2960	}
2961
2962	/*
2963	 * Add steps to execute any MULTIEXPR SubPlans appearing in the
2964	 * expression.  We need to evaluate these before any of the Params
2965	 * referencing their outputs are used, but after we've prepared for any
2966	 * Var references they may contain.  (There cannot be cross-references
2967	 * between MULTIEXPR SubPlans, so we needn't worry about their order.)
2968	 */
2969	foreach(lc, info->multiexpr_subplans)
2970	{
2971		SubPlan    *subplan = (SubPlan *) lfirst(lc);
2972
2973		Assert(subplan->subLinkType == MULTIEXPR_SUBLINK);
2974
2975		/* The result can be ignored, but we better put it somewhere */
2976		ExecInitSubPlanExpr(subplan, state,
2977							&state->resvalue, &state->resnull);
2978	}
2979}
2980
2981/*
2982 * expr_setup_walker: expression walker for ExecCreateExprSetupSteps
2983 */
2984static bool
2985expr_setup_walker(Node *node, ExprSetupInfo *info)
2986{
2987	if (node == NULL)
2988		return false;
2989	if (IsA(node, Var))
2990	{
2991		Var		   *variable = (Var *) node;
2992		AttrNumber	attnum = variable->varattno;
2993
2994		switch (variable->varno)
2995		{
2996			case INNER_VAR:
2997				info->last_inner = Max(info->last_inner, attnum);
2998				break;
2999
3000			case OUTER_VAR:
3001				info->last_outer = Max(info->last_outer, attnum);
3002				break;
3003
3004				/* INDEX_VAR is handled by default case */
3005
3006			default:
3007				switch (variable->varreturningtype)
3008				{
3009					case VAR_RETURNING_DEFAULT:
3010						info->last_scan = Max(info->last_scan, attnum);
3011						break;
3012					case VAR_RETURNING_OLD:
3013						info->last_old = Max(info->last_old, attnum);
3014						break;
3015					case VAR_RETURNING_NEW:
3016						info->last_new = Max(info->last_new, attnum);
3017						break;
3018				}
3019				break;
3020		}
3021		return false;
3022	}
3023
3024	/* Collect all MULTIEXPR SubPlans, too */
3025	if (IsA(node, SubPlan))
3026	{
3027		SubPlan    *subplan = (SubPlan *) node;
3028
3029		if (subplan->subLinkType == MULTIEXPR_SUBLINK)
3030			info->multiexpr_subplans = lappend(info->multiexpr_subplans,
3031											   subplan);
3032	}
3033
3034	/*
3035	 * Don't examine the arguments or filters of Aggrefs or WindowFuncs,
3036	 * because those do not represent expressions to be evaluated within the
3037	 * calling expression's econtext.  GroupingFunc arguments are never
3038	 * evaluated at all.
3039	 */
3040	if (IsA(node, Aggref))
3041		return false;
3042	if (IsA(node, WindowFunc))
3043		return false;
3044	if (IsA(node, GroupingFunc))
3045		return false;
3046	return expression_tree_walker(node, expr_setup_walker, info);
3047}
3048
3049/*
3050 * Compute additional information for EEOP_*_FETCHSOME ops.
3051 *
3052 * The goal is to determine whether a slot is 'fixed', that is, every
3053 * evaluation of the expression will have the same type of slot, with an
3054 * equivalent descriptor.
3055 *
3056 * EEOP_OLD_FETCHSOME and EEOP_NEW_FETCHSOME are used to process RETURNING, if
3057 * OLD/NEW columns are referred to explicitly.  In both cases, the tuple
3058 * descriptor comes from the parent scan node, so we treat them the same as
3059 * EEOP_SCAN_FETCHSOME.
3060 *
3061 * Returns true if the deforming step is required, false otherwise.
3062 */
3063static bool
3064ExecComputeSlotInfo(ExprState *state, ExprEvalStep *op)
3065{
3066	PlanState  *parent = state->parent;
3067	TupleDesc	desc = NULL;
3068	const TupleTableSlotOps *tts_ops = NULL;
3069	bool		isfixed = false;
3070	ExprEvalOp	opcode = op->opcode;
3071
3072	Assert(opcode == EEOP_INNER_FETCHSOME ||
3073		   opcode == EEOP_OUTER_FETCHSOME ||
3074		   opcode == EEOP_SCAN_FETCHSOME ||
3075		   opcode == EEOP_OLD_FETCHSOME ||
3076		   opcode == EEOP_NEW_FETCHSOME);
3077
3078	if (op->d.fetch.known_desc != NULL)
3079	{
3080		desc = op->d.fetch.known_desc;
3081		tts_ops = op->d.fetch.kind;
3082		isfixed = op->d.fetch.kind != NULL;
3083	}
3084	else if (!parent)
3085	{
3086		isfixed = false;
3087	}
3088	else if (opcode == EEOP_INNER_FETCHSOME)
3089	{
3090		PlanState  *is = innerPlanState(parent);
3091
3092		if (parent->inneropsset && !parent->inneropsfixed)
3093		{
3094			isfixed = false;
3095		}
3096		else if (parent->inneropsset && parent->innerops)
3097		{
3098			isfixed = true;
3099			tts_ops = parent->innerops;
3100			desc = ExecGetResultType(is);
3101		}
3102		else if (is)
3103		{
3104			tts_ops = ExecGetResultSlotOps(is, &isfixed);
3105			desc = ExecGetResultType(is);
3106		}
3107	}
3108	else if (opcode == EEOP_OUTER_FETCHSOME)
3109	{
3110		PlanState  *os = outerPlanState(parent);
3111
3112		if (parent->outeropsset && !parent->outeropsfixed)
3113		{
3114			isfixed = false;
3115		}
3116		else if (parent->outeropsset && parent->outerops)
3117		{
3118			isfixed = true;
3119			tts_ops = parent->outerops;
3120			desc = ExecGetResultType(os);
3121		}
3122		else if (os)
3123		{
3124			tts_ops = ExecGetResultSlotOps(os, &isfixed);
3125			desc = ExecGetResultType(os);
3126		}
3127	}
3128	else if (opcode == EEOP_SCAN_FETCHSOME ||
3129			 opcode == EEOP_OLD_FETCHSOME ||
3130			 opcode == EEOP_NEW_FETCHSOME)
3131	{
3132		desc = parent->scandesc;
3133
3134		if (parent->scanops)
3135			tts_ops = parent->scanops;
3136
3137		if (parent->scanopsset)
3138			isfixed = parent->scanopsfixed;
3139	}
3140
3141	if (isfixed && desc != NULL && tts_ops != NULL)
3142	{
3143		op->d.fetch.fixed = true;
3144		op->d.fetch.kind = tts_ops;
3145		op->d.fetch.known_desc = desc;
3146	}
3147	else
3148	{
3149		op->d.fetch.fixed = false;
3150		op->d.fetch.kind = NULL;
3151		op->d.fetch.known_desc = NULL;
3152	}
3153
3154	/* if the slot is known to always virtual we never need to deform */
3155	if (op->d.fetch.fixed && op->d.fetch.kind == &TTSOpsVirtual)
3156		return false;
3157
3158	return true;
3159}
3160
3161/*
3162 * Prepare step for the evaluation of a whole-row variable.
3163 * The caller still has to push the step.
3164 */
3165static void
3166ExecInitWholeRowVar(ExprEvalStep *scratch, Var *variable, ExprState *state)
3167{
3168	PlanState  *parent = state->parent;
3169
3170	/* fill in all but the target */
3171	scratch->opcode = EEOP_WHOLEROW;
3172	scratch->d.wholerow.var = variable;
3173	scratch->d.wholerow.first = true;
3174	scratch->d.wholerow.slow = false;
3175	scratch->d.wholerow.tupdesc = NULL; /* filled at runtime */
3176	scratch->d.wholerow.junkFilter = NULL;
3177
3178	/* update ExprState flags if Var refers to OLD/NEW */
3179	if (variable->varreturningtype == VAR_RETURNING_OLD)
3180		state->flags |= EEO_FLAG_HAS_OLD;
3181	else if (variable->varreturningtype == VAR_RETURNING_NEW)
3182		state->flags |= EEO_FLAG_HAS_NEW;
3183
3184	/*
3185	 * If the input tuple came from a subquery, it might contain "resjunk"
3186	 * columns (such as GROUP BY or ORDER BY columns), which we don't want to
3187	 * keep in the whole-row result.  We can get rid of such columns by
3188	 * passing the tuple through a JunkFilter --- but to make one, we have to
3189	 * lay our hands on the subquery's targetlist.  Fortunately, there are not
3190	 * very many cases where this can happen, and we can identify all of them
3191	 * by examining our parent PlanState.  We assume this is not an issue in
3192	 * standalone expressions that don't have parent plans.  (Whole-row Vars
3193	 * can occur in such expressions, but they will always be referencing
3194	 * table rows.)
3195	 */
3196	if (parent)
3197	{
3198		PlanState  *subplan = NULL;
3199
3200		switch (nodeTag(parent))
3201		{
3202			case T_SubqueryScanState:
3203				subplan = ((SubqueryScanState *) parent)->subplan;
3204				break;
3205			case T_CteScanState:
3206				subplan = ((CteScanState *) parent)->cteplanstate;
3207				break;
3208			default:
3209				break;
3210		}
3211
3212		if (subplan)
3213		{
3214			bool		junk_filter_needed = false;
3215			ListCell   *tlist;
3216
3217			/* Detect whether subplan tlist actually has any junk columns */
3218			foreach(tlist, subplan->plan->targetlist)
3219			{
3220				TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(tlist);
3221
3222				if (tle->resjunk)
3223				{
3224					junk_filter_needed = true;
3225					break;
3226				}
3227			}
3228
3229			/* If so, build the junkfilter now */
3230			if (junk_filter_needed)
3231			{
3232				scratch->d.wholerow.junkFilter =
3233					ExecInitJunkFilter(subplan->plan->targetlist,
3234									   ExecInitExtraTupleSlot(parent->state, NULL,
3235															  &TTSOpsVirtual));
3236			}
3237		}
3238	}
3239}
3240
3241/*
3242 * Prepare evaluation of a SubscriptingRef expression.
3243 */
3244static void
3245ExecInitSubscriptingRef(ExprEvalStep *scratch, SubscriptingRef *sbsref,
3246						ExprState *state, Datum *resv, bool *resnull)
3247{
3248	bool		isAssignment = (sbsref->refassgnexpr != NULL);
3249	int			nupper = list_length(sbsref->refupperindexpr);
3250	int			nlower = list_length(sbsref->reflowerindexpr);
3251	const SubscriptRoutines *sbsroutines;
3252	SubscriptingRefState *sbsrefstate;
3253	SubscriptExecSteps methods;
3254	char	   *ptr;
3255	List	   *adjust_jumps = NIL;
3256	ListCell   *lc;
3257	int			i;
3258
3259	/* Look up the subscripting support methods */
3260	sbsroutines = getSubscriptingRoutines(sbsref->refcontainertype, NULL);
3261	if (!sbsroutines)
3262		ereport(ERROR,
3263				(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
3264				 errmsg("cannot subscript type %s because it does not support subscripting",
3265						format_type_be(sbsref->refcontainertype)),
3266				 state->parent ?
3267				 executor_errposition(state->parent->state,
3268									  exprLocation((Node *) sbsref)) : 0));
3269
3270	/* Allocate sbsrefstate, with enough space for per-subscript arrays too */
3271	sbsrefstate = palloc0(MAXALIGN(sizeof(SubscriptingRefState)) +
3272						  (nupper + nlower) * (sizeof(Datum) +
3273											   2 * sizeof(bool)));
3274
3275	/* Fill constant fields of SubscriptingRefState */
3276	sbsrefstate->isassignment = isAssignment;
3277	sbsrefstate->numupper = nupper;
3278	sbsrefstate->numlower = nlower;
3279	/* Set up per-subscript arrays */
3280	ptr = ((char *) sbsrefstate) + MAXALIGN(sizeof(SubscriptingRefState));
3281	sbsrefstate->upperindex = (Datum *) ptr;
3282	ptr += nupper * sizeof(Datum);
3283	sbsrefstate->lowerindex = (Datum *) ptr;
3284	ptr += nlower * sizeof(Datum);
3285	sbsrefstate->upperprovided = (bool *) ptr;
3286	ptr += nupper * sizeof(bool);
3287	sbsrefstate->lowerprovided = (bool *) ptr;
3288	ptr += nlower * sizeof(bool);
3289	sbsrefstate->upperindexnull = (bool *) ptr;
3290	ptr += nupper * sizeof(bool);
3291	sbsrefstate->lowerindexnull = (bool *) ptr;
3292	/* ptr += nlower * sizeof(bool); */
3293
3294	/*
3295	 * Let the container-type-specific code have a chance.  It must fill the
3296	 * "methods" struct with function pointers for us to possibly use in
3297	 * execution steps below; and it can optionally set up some data pointed
3298	 * to by the workspace field.
3299	 */
3300	memset(&methods, 0, sizeof(methods));
3301	sbsroutines->exec_setup(sbsref, sbsrefstate, &methods);
3302
3303	/*
3304	 * Evaluate array input.  It's safe to do so into resv/resnull, because we
3305	 * won't use that as target for any of the other subexpressions, and it'll
3306	 * be overwritten by the final EEOP_SBSREF_FETCH/ASSIGN step, which is
3307	 * pushed last.
3308	 */
3309	ExecInitExprRec(sbsref->refexpr, state, resv, resnull);
3310
3311	/*
3312	 * If refexpr yields NULL, and the operation should be strict, then result
3313	 * is NULL.  We can implement this with just JUMP_IF_NULL, since we
3314	 * evaluated the array into the desired target location.
3315	 */
3316	if (!isAssignment && sbsroutines->fetch_strict)
3317	{
3318		scratch->opcode = EEOP_JUMP_IF_NULL;
3319		scratch->d.jump.jumpdone = -1;	/* adjust later */
3320		ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
3321		adjust_jumps = lappend_int(adjust_jumps,
3322								   state->steps_len - 1);
3323	}
3324
3325	/* Evaluate upper subscripts */
3326	i = 0;
3327	foreach(lc, sbsref->refupperindexpr)
3328	{
3329		Expr	   *e = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
3330
3331		/* When slicing, individual subscript bounds can be omitted */
3332		if (!e)
3333		{
3334			sbsrefstate->upperprovided[i] = false;
3335			sbsrefstate->upperindexnull[i] = true;
3336		}
3337		else
3338		{
3339			sbsrefstate->upperprovided[i] = true;
3340			/* Each subscript is evaluated into appropriate array entry */
3341			ExecInitExprRec(e, state,
3342							&sbsrefstate->upperindex[i],
3343							&sbsrefstate->upperindexnull[i]);
3344		}
3345		i++;
3346	}
3347
3348	/* Evaluate lower subscripts similarly */
3349	i = 0;
3350	foreach(lc, sbsref->reflowerindexpr)
3351	{
3352		Expr	   *e = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
3353
3354		/* When slicing, individual subscript bounds can be omitted */
3355		if (!e)
3356		{
3357			sbsrefstate->lowerprovided[i] = false;
3358			sbsrefstate->lowerindexnull[i] = true;
3359		}
3360		else
3361		{
3362			sbsrefstate->lowerprovided[i] = true;
3363			/* Each subscript is evaluated into appropriate array entry */
3364			ExecInitExprRec(e, state,
3365							&sbsrefstate->lowerindex[i],
3366							&sbsrefstate->lowerindexnull[i]);
3367		}
3368		i++;
3369	}
3370
3371	/* SBSREF_SUBSCRIPTS checks and converts all the subscripts at once */
3372	if (methods.sbs_check_subscripts)
3373	{
3374		scratch->opcode = EEOP_SBSREF_SUBSCRIPTS;
3375		scratch->d.sbsref_subscript.subscriptfunc = methods.sbs_check_subscripts;
3376		scratch->d.sbsref_subscript.state = sbsrefstate;
3377		scratch->d.sbsref_subscript.jumpdone = -1;	/* adjust later */
3378		ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
3379		adjust_jumps = lappend_int(adjust_jumps,
3380								   state->steps_len - 1);
3381	}
3382
3383	if (isAssignment)
3384	{
3385		Datum	   *save_innermost_caseval;
3386		bool	   *save_innermost_casenull;
3387
3388		/* Check for unimplemented methods */
3389		if (!methods.sbs_assign)
3390			ereport(ERROR,
3391					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3392					 errmsg("type %s does not support subscripted assignment",
3393							format_type_be(sbsref->refcontainertype))));
3394
3395		/*
3396		 * We might have a nested-assignment situation, in which the
3397		 * refassgnexpr is itself a FieldStore or SubscriptingRef that needs
3398		 * to obtain and modify the previous value of the array element or
3399		 * slice being replaced.  If so, we have to extract that value from
3400		 * the array and pass it down via the CaseTestExpr mechanism.  It's
3401		 * safe to reuse the CASE mechanism because there cannot be a CASE
3402		 * between here and where the value would be needed, and an array
3403		 * assignment can't be within a CASE either.  (So saving and restoring
3404		 * innermost_caseval is just paranoia, but let's do it anyway.)
3405		 *
3406		 * Since fetching the old element might be a nontrivial expense, do it
3407		 * only if the argument actually needs it.
3408		 */
3409		if (isAssignmentIndirectionExpr(sbsref->refassgnexpr))
3410		{
3411			if (!methods.sbs_fetch_old)
3412				ereport(ERROR,
3413						(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3414						 errmsg("type %s does not support subscripted assignment",
3415								format_type_be(sbsref->refcontainertype))));
3416			scratch->opcode = EEOP_SBSREF_OLD;
3417			scratch->d.sbsref.subscriptfunc = methods.sbs_fetch_old;
3418			scratch->d.sbsref.state = sbsrefstate;
3419			ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
3420		}
3421
3422		/* SBSREF_OLD puts extracted value into prevvalue/prevnull */
3423		save_innermost_caseval = state->innermost_caseval;
3424		save_innermost_casenull = state->innermost_casenull;
3425		state->innermost_caseval = &sbsrefstate->prevvalue;
3426		state->innermost_casenull = &sbsrefstate->prevnull;
3427
3428		/* evaluate replacement value into replacevalue/replacenull */
3429		ExecInitExprRec(sbsref->refassgnexpr, state,
3430						&sbsrefstate->replacevalue, &sbsrefstate->replacenull);
3431
3432		state->innermost_caseval = save_innermost_caseval;
3433		state->innermost_casenull = save_innermost_casenull;
3434
3435		/* and perform the assignment */
3436		scratch->opcode = EEOP_SBSREF_ASSIGN;
3437		scratch->d.sbsref.subscriptfunc = methods.sbs_assign;
3438		scratch->d.sbsref.state = sbsrefstate;
3439		ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
3440	}
3441	else
3442	{
3443		/* array fetch is much simpler */
3444		scratch->opcode = EEOP_SBSREF_FETCH;
3445		scratch->d.sbsref.subscriptfunc = methods.sbs_fetch;
3446		scratch->d.sbsref.state = sbsrefstate;
3447		ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
3448	}
3449
3450	/* adjust jump targets */
3451	foreach(lc, adjust_jumps)
3452	{
3453		ExprEvalStep *as = &state->steps[lfirst_int(lc)];
3454
3455		if (as->opcode == EEOP_SBSREF_SUBSCRIPTS)
3456		{
3457			Assert(as->d.sbsref_subscript.jumpdone == -1);
3458			as->d.sbsref_subscript.jumpdone = state->steps_len;
3459		}
3460		else
3461		{
3462			Assert(as->opcode == EEOP_JUMP_IF_NULL);
3463			Assert(as->d.jump.jumpdone == -1);
3464			as->d.jump.jumpdone = state->steps_len;
3465		}
3466	}
3467}
3468
3469/*
3470 * Helper for preparing SubscriptingRef expressions for evaluation: is expr
3471 * a nested FieldStore or SubscriptingRef that needs the old element value
3472 * passed down?
3473 *
3474 * (We could use this in FieldStore too, but in that case passing the old
3475 * value is so cheap there's no need.)
3476 *
3477 * Note: it might seem that this needs to recurse, but in most cases it does
3478 * not; the CaseTestExpr, if any, will be directly the arg or refexpr of the
3479 * top-level node.  Nested-assignment situations give rise to expression
3480 * trees in which each level of assignment has its own CaseTestExpr, and the
3481 * recursive structure appears within the newvals or refassgnexpr field.
3482 * There is an exception, though: if the array is an array-of-domain, we will
3483 * have a CoerceToDomain or RelabelType as the refassgnexpr, and we need to
3484 * be able to look through that.
3485 */
3486static bool
3487isAssignmentIndirectionExpr(Expr *expr)
3488{
3489	if (expr == NULL)
3490		return false;			/* just paranoia */
3491	if (IsA(expr, FieldStore))
3492	{
3493		FieldStore *fstore = (FieldStore *) expr;
3494
3495		if (fstore->arg && IsA(fstore->arg, CaseTestExpr))
3496			return true;
3497	}
3498	else if (IsA(expr, SubscriptingRef))
3499	{
3500		SubscriptingRef *sbsRef = (SubscriptingRef *) expr;
3501
3502		if (sbsRef->refexpr && IsA(sbsRef->refexpr, CaseTestExpr))
3503			return true;
3504	}
3505	else if (IsA(expr, CoerceToDomain))
3506	{
3507		CoerceToDomain *cd = (CoerceToDomain *) expr;
3508
3509		return isAssignmentIndirectionExpr(cd->arg);
3510	}
3511	else if (IsA(expr, RelabelType))
3512	{
3513		RelabelType *r = (RelabelType *) expr;
3514
3515		return isAssignmentIndirectionExpr(r->arg);
3516	}
3517	return false;
3518}
3519
3520/*
3521 * Prepare evaluation of a CoerceToDomain expression.
3522 */
3523static void
3524ExecInitCoerceToDomain(ExprEvalStep *scratch, CoerceToDomain *ctest,
3525					   ExprState *state, Datum *resv, bool *resnull)
3526{
3527	DomainConstraintRef *constraint_ref;
3528	Datum	   *domainval = NULL;
3529	bool	   *domainnull = NULL;
3530	ListCell   *l;
3531
3532	scratch->d.domaincheck.resulttype = ctest->resulttype;
3533	/* we'll allocate workspace only if needed */
3534	scratch->d.domaincheck.checkvalue = NULL;
3535	scratch->d.domaincheck.checknull = NULL;
3536	scratch->d.domaincheck.escontext = state->escontext;
3537
3538	/*
3539	 * Evaluate argument - it's fine to directly store it into resv/resnull,
3540	 * if there's constraint failures there'll be errors, otherwise it's what
3541	 * needs to be returned.
3542	 */
3543	ExecInitExprRec(ctest->arg, state, resv, resnull);
3544
3545	/*
3546	 * Note: if the argument is of varlena type, it could be a R/W expanded
3547	 * object.  We want to return the R/W pointer as the final result, but we
3548	 * have to pass a R/O pointer as the value to be tested by any functions
3549	 * in check expressions.  We don't bother to emit a MAKE_READONLY step
3550	 * unless there's actually at least one check expression, though.  Until
3551	 * we've tested that, domainval/domainnull are NULL.
3552	 */
3553
3554	/*
3555	 * Collect the constraints associated with the domain.
3556	 *
3557	 * Note: before PG v10 we'd recheck the set of constraints during each
3558	 * evaluation of the expression.  Now we bake them into the ExprState
3559	 * during executor initialization.  That means we don't need typcache.c to
3560	 * provide compiled exprs.
3561	 */
3562	constraint_ref = (DomainConstraintRef *)
3563		palloc(sizeof(DomainConstraintRef));
3564	InitDomainConstraintRef(ctest->resulttype,
3565							constraint_ref,
3566							CurrentMemoryContext,
3567							false);
3568
3569	/*
3570	 * Compile code to check each domain constraint.  NOTNULL constraints can
3571	 * just be applied on the resv/resnull value, but for CHECK constraints we
3572	 * need more pushups.
3573	 */
3574	foreach(l, constraint_ref->constraints)
3575	{
3576		DomainConstraintState *con = (DomainConstraintState *) lfirst(l);
3577		Datum	   *save_innermost_domainval;
3578		bool	   *save_innermost_domainnull;
3579
3580		scratch->d.domaincheck.constraintname = con->name;
3581
3582		switch (con->constrainttype)
3583		{
3584			case DOM_CONSTRAINT_NOTNULL:
3585				scratch->opcode = EEOP_DOMAIN_NOTNULL;
3586				ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
3587				break;
3588			case DOM_CONSTRAINT_CHECK:
3589				/* Allocate workspace for CHECK output if we didn't yet */
3590				if (scratch->d.domaincheck.checkvalue == NULL)
3591				{
3592					scratch->d.domaincheck.checkvalue =
3593						(Datum *) palloc(sizeof(Datum));
3594					scratch->d.domaincheck.checknull =
3595						(bool *) palloc(sizeof(bool));
3596				}
3597
3598				/*
3599				 * If first time through, determine where CoerceToDomainValue
3600				 * nodes should read from.
3601				 */
3602				if (domainval == NULL)
3603				{
3604					/*
3605					 * Since value might be read multiple times, force to R/O
3606					 * - but only if it could be an expanded datum.
3607					 */
3608					if (get_typlen(ctest->resulttype) == -1)
3609					{
3610						ExprEvalStep scratch2 = {0};
3611
3612						/* Yes, so make output workspace for MAKE_READONLY */
3613						domainval = (Datum *) palloc(sizeof(Datum));
3614						domainnull = (bool *) palloc(sizeof(bool));
3615
3616						/* Emit MAKE_READONLY */
3617						scratch2.opcode = EEOP_MAKE_READONLY;
3618						scratch2.resvalue = domainval;
3619						scratch2.resnull = domainnull;
3620						scratch2.d.make_readonly.value = resv;
3621						scratch2.d.make_readonly.isnull = resnull;
3622						ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch2);
3623					}
3624					else
3625					{
3626						/* No, so it's fine to read from resv/resnull */
3627						domainval = resv;
3628						domainnull = resnull;
3629					}
3630				}
3631
3632				/*
3633				 * Set up value to be returned by CoerceToDomainValue nodes.
3634				 * We must save and restore innermost_domainval/null fields,
3635				 * in case this node is itself within a check expression for
3636				 * another domain.
3637				 */
3638				save_innermost_domainval = state->innermost_domainval;
3639				save_innermost_domainnull = state->innermost_domainnull;
3640				state->innermost_domainval = domainval;
3641				state->innermost_domainnull = domainnull;
3642
3643				/* evaluate check expression value */
3644				ExecInitExprRec(con->check_expr, state,
3645								scratch->d.domaincheck.checkvalue,
3646								scratch->d.domaincheck.checknull);
3647
3648				state->innermost_domainval = save_innermost_domainval;
3649				state->innermost_domainnull = save_innermost_domainnull;
3650
3651				/* now test result */
3652				scratch->opcode = EEOP_DOMAIN_CHECK;
3653				ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
3654
3655				break;
3656			default:
3657				elog(ERROR, "unrecognized constraint type: %d",
3658					 (int) con->constrainttype);
3659				break;
3660		}
3661	}
3662}
3663
3664/*
3665 * Build transition/combine function invocations for all aggregate transition
3666 * / combination function invocations in a grouping sets phase. This has to
3667 * invoke all sort based transitions in a phase (if doSort is true), all hash
3668 * based transitions (if doHash is true), or both (both true).
3669 *
3670 * The resulting expression will, for each set of transition values, first
3671 * check for filters, evaluate aggregate input, check that that input is not
3672 * NULL for a strict transition function, and then finally invoke the
3673 * transition for each of the concurrently computed grouping sets.
3674 *
3675 * If nullcheck is true, the generated code will check for a NULL pointer to
3676 * the array of AggStatePerGroup, and skip evaluation if so.
3677 */
3678ExprState *
3679ExecBuildAggTrans(AggState *aggstate, AggStatePerPhase phase,
3680				  bool doSort, bool doHash, bool nullcheck)
3681{
3682	ExprState  *state = makeNode(ExprState);
3683	PlanState  *parent = &aggstate->ss.ps;
3684	ExprEvalStep scratch = {0};
3685	bool		isCombine = DO_AGGSPLIT_COMBINE(aggstate->aggsplit);
3686	ExprSetupInfo deform = {0, 0, 0, 0, 0, NIL};
3687
3688	state->expr = (Expr *) aggstate;
3689	state->parent = parent;
3690
3691	scratch.resvalue = &state->resvalue;
3692	scratch.resnull = &state->resnull;
3693
3694	/*
3695	 * First figure out which slots, and how many columns from each, we're
3696	 * going to need.
3697	 */
3698	for (int transno = 0; transno < aggstate->numtrans; transno++)
3699	{
3700		AggStatePerTrans pertrans = &aggstate->pertrans[transno];
3701
3702		expr_setup_walker((Node *) pertrans->aggref->aggdirectargs,
3703						  &deform);
3704		expr_setup_walker((Node *) pertrans->aggref->args,
3705						  &deform);
3706		expr_setup_walker((Node *) pertrans->aggref->aggorder,
3707						  &deform);
3708		expr_setup_walker((Node *) pertrans->aggref->aggdistinct,
3709						  &deform);
3710		expr_setup_walker((Node *) pertrans->aggref->aggfilter,
3711						  &deform);
3712	}
3713	ExecPushExprSetupSteps(state, &deform);
3714
3715	/*
3716	 * Emit instructions for each transition value / grouping set combination.
3717	 */
3718	for (int transno = 0; transno < aggstate->numtrans; transno++)
3719	{
3720		AggStatePerTrans pertrans = &aggstate->pertrans[transno];
3721		FunctionCallInfo trans_fcinfo = pertrans->transfn_fcinfo;
3722		List	   *adjust_bailout = NIL;
3723		NullableDatum *strictargs = NULL;
3724		bool	   *strictnulls = NULL;
3725		int			argno;
3726		ListCell   *bail;
3727
3728		/*
3729		 * If filter present, emit. Do so before evaluating the input, to
3730		 * avoid potentially unneeded computations, or even worse, unintended
3731		 * side-effects.  When combining, all the necessary filtering has
3732		 * already been done.
3733		 */
3734		if (pertrans->aggref->aggfilter && !isCombine)
3735		{
3736			/* evaluate filter expression */
3737			ExecInitExprRec(pertrans->aggref->aggfilter, state,
3738							&state->resvalue, &state->resnull);
3739			/* and jump out if false */
3740			scratch.opcode = EEOP_JUMP_IF_NOT_TRUE;
3741			scratch.d.jump.jumpdone = -1;	/* adjust later */
3742			ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
3743			adjust_bailout = lappend_int(adjust_bailout,
3744										 state->steps_len - 1);
3745		}
3746
3747		/*
3748		 * Evaluate arguments to aggregate/combine function.
3749		 */
3750		argno = 0;
3751		if (isCombine)
3752		{
3753			/*
3754			 * Combining two aggregate transition values. Instead of directly
3755			 * coming from a tuple the input is a, potentially deserialized,
3756			 * transition value.
3757			 */
3758			TargetEntry *source_tle;
3759
3760			Assert(pertrans->numSortCols == 0);
3761			Assert(list_length(pertrans->aggref->args) == 1);
3762
3763			strictargs = trans_fcinfo->args + 1;
3764			source_tle = (TargetEntry *) linitial(pertrans->aggref->args);
3765
3766			/*
3767			 * deserialfn_oid will be set if we must deserialize the input
3768			 * state before calling the combine function.
3769			 */
3770			if (!OidIsValid(pertrans->deserialfn_oid))
3771			{
3772				/*
3773				 * Start from 1, since the 0th arg will be the transition
3774				 * value
3775				 */
3776				ExecInitExprRec(source_tle->expr, state,
3777								&trans_fcinfo->args[argno + 1].value,
3778								&trans_fcinfo->args[argno + 1].isnull);
3779			}
3780			else
3781			{
3782				FunctionCallInfo ds_fcinfo = pertrans->deserialfn_fcinfo;
3783
3784				/* evaluate argument */
3785				ExecInitExprRec(source_tle->expr, state,
3786								&ds_fcinfo->args[0].value,
3787								&ds_fcinfo->args[0].isnull);
3788
3789				/* Dummy second argument for type-safety reasons */
3790				ds_fcinfo->args[1].value = PointerGetDatum(NULL);
3791				ds_fcinfo->args[1].isnull = false;
3792
3793				/*
3794				 * Don't call a strict deserialization function with NULL
3795				 * input
3796				 */
3797				if (pertrans->deserialfn.fn_strict)
3798					scratch.opcode = EEOP_AGG_STRICT_DESERIALIZE;
3799				else
3800					scratch.opcode = EEOP_AGG_DESERIALIZE;
3801
3802				scratch.d.agg_deserialize.fcinfo_data = ds_fcinfo;
3803				scratch.d.agg_deserialize.jumpnull = -1;	/* adjust later */
3804				scratch.resvalue = &trans_fcinfo->args[argno + 1].value;
3805				scratch.resnull = &trans_fcinfo->args[argno + 1].isnull;
3806
3807				ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
3808				/* don't add an adjustment unless the function is strict */
3809				if (pertrans->deserialfn.fn_strict)
3810					adjust_bailout = lappend_int(adjust_bailout,
3811												 state->steps_len - 1);
3812
3813				/* restore normal settings of scratch fields */
3814				scratch.resvalue = &state->resvalue;
3815				scratch.resnull = &state->resnull;
3816			}
3817			argno++;
3818
3819			Assert(pertrans->numInputs == argno);
3820		}
3821		else if (!pertrans->aggsortrequired)
3822		{
3823			ListCell   *arg;
3824
3825			/*
3826			 * Normal transition function without ORDER BY / DISTINCT or with
3827			 * ORDER BY / DISTINCT but the planner has given us pre-sorted
3828			 * input.
3829			 */
3830			strictargs = trans_fcinfo->args + 1;
3831
3832			foreach(arg, pertrans->aggref->args)
3833			{
3834				TargetEntry *source_tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(arg);
3835
3836				/*
3837				 * Don't initialize args for any ORDER BY clause that might
3838				 * exist in a presorted aggregate.
3839				 */
3840				if (argno == pertrans->numTransInputs)
3841					break;
3842
3843				/*
3844				 * Start from 1, since the 0th arg will be the transition
3845				 * value
3846				 */
3847				ExecInitExprRec(source_tle->expr, state,
3848								&trans_fcinfo->args[argno + 1].value,
3849								&trans_fcinfo->args[argno + 1].isnull);
3850				argno++;
3851			}
3852			Assert(pertrans->numTransInputs == argno);
3853		}
3854		else if (pertrans->numInputs == 1)
3855		{
3856			/*
3857			 * Non-presorted DISTINCT and/or ORDER BY case, with a single
3858			 * column sorted on.
3859			 */
3860			TargetEntry *source_tle =
3861				(TargetEntry *) linitial(pertrans->aggref->args);
3862
3863			Assert(list_length(pertrans->aggref->args) == 1);
3864
3865			ExecInitExprRec(source_tle->expr, state,
3866							&state->resvalue,
3867							&state->resnull);
3868			strictnulls = &state->resnull;
3869			argno++;
3870
3871			Assert(pertrans->numInputs == argno);
3872		}
3873		else
3874		{
3875			/*
3876			 * Non-presorted DISTINCT and/or ORDER BY case, with multiple
3877			 * columns sorted on.
3878			 */
3879			Datum	   *values = pertrans->sortslot->tts_values;
3880			bool	   *nulls = pertrans->sortslot->tts_isnull;
3881			ListCell   *arg;
3882
3883			strictnulls = nulls;
3884
3885			foreach(arg, pertrans->aggref->args)
3886			{
3887				TargetEntry *source_tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(arg);
3888
3889				ExecInitExprRec(source_tle->expr, state,
3890								&values[argno], &nulls[argno]);
3891				argno++;
3892			}
3893			Assert(pertrans->numInputs == argno);
3894		}
3895
3896		/*
3897		 * For a strict transfn, nothing happens when there's a NULL input; we
3898		 * just keep the prior transValue. This is true for both plain and
3899		 * sorted/distinct aggregates.
3900		 */
3901		if (trans_fcinfo->flinfo->fn_strict && pertrans->numTransInputs > 0)
3902		{
3903			if (strictnulls)
3904				scratch.opcode = EEOP_AGG_STRICT_INPUT_CHECK_NULLS;
3905			else if (strictargs && pertrans->numTransInputs == 1)
3906				scratch.opcode = EEOP_AGG_STRICT_INPUT_CHECK_ARGS_1;
3907			else
3908				scratch.opcode = EEOP_AGG_STRICT_INPUT_CHECK_ARGS;
3909			scratch.d.agg_strict_input_check.nulls = strictnulls;
3910			scratch.d.agg_strict_input_check.args = strictargs;
3911			scratch.d.agg_strict_input_check.jumpnull = -1; /* adjust later */
3912			scratch.d.agg_strict_input_check.nargs = pertrans->numTransInputs;
3913			ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
3914			adjust_bailout = lappend_int(adjust_bailout,
3915										 state->steps_len - 1);
3916		}
3917
3918		/* Handle DISTINCT aggregates which have pre-sorted input */
3919		if (pertrans->numDistinctCols > 0 && !pertrans->aggsortrequired)
3920		{
3921			if (pertrans->numDistinctCols > 1)
3922				scratch.opcode = EEOP_AGG_PRESORTED_DISTINCT_MULTI;
3923			else
3924				scratch.opcode = EEOP_AGG_PRESORTED_DISTINCT_SINGLE;
3925
3926			scratch.d.agg_presorted_distinctcheck.pertrans = pertrans;
3927			scratch.d.agg_presorted_distinctcheck.jumpdistinct = -1;	/* adjust later */
3928			ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
3929			adjust_bailout = lappend_int(adjust_bailout,
3930										 state->steps_len - 1);
3931		}
3932
3933		/*
3934		 * Call transition function (once for each concurrently evaluated
3935		 * grouping set). Do so for both sort and hash based computations, as
3936		 * applicable.
3937		 */
3938		if (doSort)
3939		{
3940			int			processGroupingSets = Max(phase->numsets, 1);
3941			int			setoff = 0;
3942
3943			for (int setno = 0; setno < processGroupingSets; setno++)
3944			{
3945				ExecBuildAggTransCall(state, aggstate, &scratch, trans_fcinfo,
3946									  pertrans, transno, setno, setoff, false,
3947									  nullcheck);
3948				setoff++;
3949			}
3950		}
3951
3952		if (doHash)
3953		{
3954			int			numHashes = aggstate->num_hashes;
3955			int			setoff;
3956
3957			/* in MIXED mode, there'll be preceding transition values */
3958			if (aggstate->aggstrategy != AGG_HASHED)
3959				setoff = aggstate->maxsets;
3960			else
3961				setoff = 0;
3962
3963			for (int setno = 0; setno < numHashes; setno++)
3964			{
3965				ExecBuildAggTransCall(state, aggstate, &scratch, trans_fcinfo,
3966									  pertrans, transno, setno, setoff, true,
3967									  nullcheck);
3968				setoff++;
3969			}
3970		}
3971
3972		/* adjust early bail out jump target(s) */
3973		foreach(bail, adjust_bailout)
3974		{
3975			ExprEvalStep *as = &state->steps[lfirst_int(bail)];
3976
3977			if (as->opcode == EEOP_JUMP_IF_NOT_TRUE)
3978			{
3979				Assert(as->d.jump.jumpdone == -1);
3980				as->d.jump.jumpdone = state->steps_len;
3981			}
3982			else if (as->opcode == EEOP_AGG_STRICT_INPUT_CHECK_ARGS ||
3983					 as->opcode == EEOP_AGG_STRICT_INPUT_CHECK_ARGS_1 ||
3984					 as->opcode == EEOP_AGG_STRICT_INPUT_CHECK_NULLS)
3985			{
3986				Assert(as->d.agg_strict_input_check.jumpnull == -1);
3987				as->d.agg_strict_input_check.jumpnull = state->steps_len;
3988			}
3989			else if (as->opcode == EEOP_AGG_STRICT_DESERIALIZE)
3990			{
3991				Assert(as->d.agg_deserialize.jumpnull == -1);
3992				as->d.agg_deserialize.jumpnull = state->steps_len;
3993			}
3994			else if (as->opcode == EEOP_AGG_PRESORTED_DISTINCT_SINGLE ||
3995					 as->opcode == EEOP_AGG_PRESORTED_DISTINCT_MULTI)
3996			{
3997				Assert(as->d.agg_presorted_distinctcheck.jumpdistinct == -1);
3998				as->d.agg_presorted_distinctcheck.jumpdistinct = state->steps_len;
3999			}
4000			else
4001				Assert(false);
4002		}
4003	}
4004
4005	scratch.resvalue = NULL;
4006	scratch.resnull = NULL;
4007	scratch.opcode = EEOP_DONE_NO_RETURN;
4008	ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4009
4010	ExecReadyExpr(state);
4011
4012	return state;
4013}
4014
4015/*
4016 * Build transition/combine function invocation for a single transition
4017 * value. This is separated from ExecBuildAggTrans() because there are
4018 * multiple callsites (hash and sort in some grouping set cases).
4019 */
4020static void
4021ExecBuildAggTransCall(ExprState *state, AggState *aggstate,
4022					  ExprEvalStep *scratch,
4023					  FunctionCallInfo fcinfo, AggStatePerTrans pertrans,
4024					  int transno, int setno, int setoff, bool ishash,
4025					  bool nullcheck)
4026{
4027	ExprContext *aggcontext;
4028	int			adjust_jumpnull = -1;
4029
4030	if (ishash)
4031		aggcontext = aggstate->hashcontext;
4032	else
4033		aggcontext = aggstate->aggcontexts[setno];
4034
4035	/* add check for NULL pointer? */
4036	if (nullcheck)
4037	{
4038		scratch->opcode = EEOP_AGG_PLAIN_PERGROUP_NULLCHECK;
4039		scratch->d.agg_plain_pergroup_nullcheck.setoff = setoff;
4040		/* adjust later */
4041		scratch->d.agg_plain_pergroup_nullcheck.jumpnull = -1;
4042		ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
4043		adjust_jumpnull = state->steps_len - 1;
4044	}
4045
4046	/*
4047	 * Determine appropriate transition implementation.
4048	 *
4049	 * For non-ordered aggregates and ORDER BY / DISTINCT aggregates with
4050	 * presorted input:
4051	 *
4052	 * If the initial value for the transition state doesn't exist in the
4053	 * pg_aggregate table then we will let the first non-NULL value returned
4054	 * from the outer procNode become the initial value. (This is useful for
4055	 * aggregates like max() and min().) The noTransValue flag signals that we
4056	 * need to do so. If true, generate a
4057	 * EEOP_AGG_INIT_STRICT_PLAIN_TRANS{,_BYVAL} step. This step also needs to
4058	 * do the work described next:
4059	 *
4060	 * If the function is strict, but does have an initial value, choose
4061	 * EEOP_AGG_STRICT_PLAIN_TRANS{,_BYVAL}, which skips the transition
4062	 * function if the transition value has become NULL (because a previous
4063	 * transition function returned NULL). This step also needs to do the work
4064	 * described next:
4065	 *
4066	 * Otherwise we call EEOP_AGG_PLAIN_TRANS{,_BYVAL}, which does not have to
4067	 * perform either of the above checks.
4068	 *
4069	 * Having steps with overlapping responsibilities is not nice, but
4070	 * aggregations are very performance sensitive, making this worthwhile.
4071	 *
4072	 * For ordered aggregates:
4073	 *
4074	 * Only need to choose between the faster path for a single ordered
4075	 * column, and the one between multiple columns. Checking strictness etc
4076	 * is done when finalizing the aggregate. See
4077	 * process_ordered_aggregate_{single, multi} and
4078	 * advance_transition_function.
4079	 */
4080	if (!pertrans->aggsortrequired)
4081	{
4082		if (pertrans->transtypeByVal)
4083		{
4084			if (fcinfo->flinfo->fn_strict &&
4085				pertrans->initValueIsNull)
4086				scratch->opcode = EEOP_AGG_PLAIN_TRANS_INIT_STRICT_BYVAL;
4087			else if (fcinfo->flinfo->fn_strict)
4088				scratch->opcode = EEOP_AGG_PLAIN_TRANS_STRICT_BYVAL;
4089			else
4090				scratch->opcode = EEOP_AGG_PLAIN_TRANS_BYVAL;
4091		}
4092		else
4093		{
4094			if (fcinfo->flinfo->fn_strict &&
4095				pertrans->initValueIsNull)
4096				scratch->opcode = EEOP_AGG_PLAIN_TRANS_INIT_STRICT_BYREF;
4097			else if (fcinfo->flinfo->fn_strict)
4098				scratch->opcode = EEOP_AGG_PLAIN_TRANS_STRICT_BYREF;
4099			else
4100				scratch->opcode = EEOP_AGG_PLAIN_TRANS_BYREF;
4101		}
4102	}
4103	else if (pertrans->numInputs == 1)
4104		scratch->opcode = EEOP_AGG_ORDERED_TRANS_DATUM;
4105	else
4106		scratch->opcode = EEOP_AGG_ORDERED_TRANS_TUPLE;
4107
4108	scratch->d.agg_trans.pertrans = pertrans;
4109	scratch->d.agg_trans.setno = setno;
4110	scratch->d.agg_trans.setoff = setoff;
4111	scratch->d.agg_trans.transno = transno;
4112	scratch->d.agg_trans.aggcontext = aggcontext;
4113	ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
4114
4115	/* fix up jumpnull */
4116	if (adjust_jumpnull != -1)
4117	{
4118		ExprEvalStep *as = &state->steps[adjust_jumpnull];
4119
4120		Assert(as->opcode == EEOP_AGG_PLAIN_PERGROUP_NULLCHECK);
4121		Assert(as->d.agg_plain_pergroup_nullcheck.jumpnull == -1);
4122		as->d.agg_plain_pergroup_nullcheck.jumpnull = state->steps_len;
4123	}
4124}
4125
4126/*
4127 * Build an ExprState that calls the given hash function(s) on the attnums
4128 * given by 'keyColIdx' .  When numCols > 1, the hash values returned by each
4129 * hash function are combined to produce a single hash value.
4130 *
4131 * desc: tuple descriptor for the to-be-hashed columns
4132 * ops: TupleTableSlotOps to use for the give TupleDesc
4133 * hashfunctions: FmgrInfos for each hash function to call, one per numCols.
4134 * These are used directly in the returned ExprState so must remain allocated.
4135 * collations: collation to use when calling the hash function.
4136 * numCols: array length of hashfunctions, collations and keyColIdx.
4137 * parent: PlanState node that the resulting ExprState will be evaluated at
4138 * init_value: Normally 0, but can be set to other values to seed the hash
4139 * with.  Non-zero is marginally slower, so best to only use if it's provably
4140 * worthwhile.
4141 */
4142ExprState *
4143ExecBuildHash32FromAttrs(TupleDesc desc, const TupleTableSlotOps *ops,
4144						 FmgrInfo *hashfunctions, Oid *collations,
4145						 int numCols, AttrNumber *keyColIdx,
4146						 PlanState *parent, uint32 init_value)
4147{
4148	ExprState  *state = makeNode(ExprState);
4149	ExprEvalStep scratch = {0};
4150	NullableDatum *iresult = NULL;
4151	intptr_t	opcode;
4152	AttrNumber	last_attnum = 0;
4153
4154	Assert(numCols >= 0);
4155
4156	state->parent = parent;
4157
4158	/*
4159	 * Make a place to store intermediate hash values between subsequent
4160	 * hashing of individual columns.  We only need this if there is more than
4161	 * one column to hash or an initial value plus one column.
4162	 */
4163	if ((int64) numCols + (init_value != 0) > 1)
4164		iresult = palloc(sizeof(NullableDatum));
4165
4166	/* find the highest attnum so we deform the tuple to that point */
4167	for (int i = 0; i < numCols; i++)
4168		last_attnum = Max(last_attnum, keyColIdx[i]);
4169
4170	scratch.opcode = EEOP_INNER_FETCHSOME;
4171	scratch.d.fetch.last_var = last_attnum;
4172	scratch.d.fetch.fixed = false;
4173	scratch.d.fetch.kind = ops;
4174	scratch.d.fetch.known_desc = desc;
4175	if (ExecComputeSlotInfo(state, &scratch))
4176		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4177
4178	if (init_value == 0)
4179	{
4180		/*
4181		 * No initial value, so we can assign the result of the hash function
4182		 * for the first attribute without having to concern ourselves with
4183		 * combining the result with any initial value.
4184		 */
4185		opcode = EEOP_HASHDATUM_FIRST;
4186	}
4187	else
4188	{
4189		/*
4190		 * Set up operation to set the initial value.  Normally we store this
4191		 * in the intermediate hash value location, but if there are no
4192		 * columns to hash, store it in the ExprState's result field.
4193		 */
4194		scratch.opcode = EEOP_HASHDATUM_SET_INITVAL;
4195		scratch.d.hashdatum_initvalue.init_value = UInt32GetDatum(init_value);
4196		scratch.resvalue = numCols > 0 ? &iresult->value : &state->resvalue;
4197		scratch.resnull = numCols > 0 ? &iresult->isnull : &state->resnull;
4198
4199		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4200
4201		/*
4202		 * When using an initial value use the NEXT32 ops as the FIRST ops
4203		 * would overwrite the stored initial value.
4204		 */
4205		opcode = EEOP_HASHDATUM_NEXT32;
4206	}
4207
4208	for (int i = 0; i < numCols; i++)
4209	{
4210		FmgrInfo   *finfo;
4211		FunctionCallInfo fcinfo;
4212		Oid			inputcollid = collations[i];
4213		AttrNumber	attnum = keyColIdx[i] - 1;
4214
4215		finfo = &hashfunctions[i];
4216		fcinfo = palloc0(SizeForFunctionCallInfo(1));
4217
4218		/* Initialize function call parameter structure too */
4219		InitFunctionCallInfoData(*fcinfo, finfo, 1, inputcollid, NULL, NULL);
4220
4221		/*
4222		 * Fetch inner Var for this attnum and store it in the 1st arg of the
4223		 * hash func.
4224		 */
4225		scratch.opcode = EEOP_INNER_VAR;
4226		scratch.resvalue = &fcinfo->args[0].value;
4227		scratch.resnull = &fcinfo->args[0].isnull;
4228		scratch.d.var.attnum = attnum;
4229		scratch.d.var.vartype = TupleDescAttr(desc, attnum)->atttypid;
4230		scratch.d.var.varreturningtype = VAR_RETURNING_DEFAULT;
4231
4232		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4233
4234		/* Call the hash function */
4235		scratch.opcode = opcode;
4236
4237		if (i == numCols - 1)
4238		{
4239			/*
4240			 * The result for hashing the final column is stored in the
4241			 * ExprState.
4242			 */
4243			scratch.resvalue = &state->resvalue;
4244			scratch.resnull = &state->resnull;
4245		}
4246		else
4247		{
4248			Assert(iresult != NULL);
4249
4250			/* intermediate values are stored in an intermediate result */
4251			scratch.resvalue = &iresult->value;
4252			scratch.resnull = &iresult->isnull;
4253		}
4254
4255		/*
4256		 * NEXT32 opcodes need to look at the intermediate result.  We might
4257		 * as well just set this for all ops.  FIRSTs won't look at it.
4258		 */
4259		scratch.d.hashdatum.iresult = iresult;
4260
4261		scratch.d.hashdatum.finfo = finfo;
4262		scratch.d.hashdatum.fcinfo_data = fcinfo;
4263		scratch.d.hashdatum.fn_addr = finfo->fn_addr;
4264		scratch.d.hashdatum.jumpdone = -1;
4265
4266		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4267
4268		/* subsequent attnums must be combined with the previous */
4269		opcode = EEOP_HASHDATUM_NEXT32;
4270	}
4271
4272	scratch.resvalue = NULL;
4273	scratch.resnull = NULL;
4274	scratch.opcode = EEOP_DONE_RETURN;
4275	ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4276
4277	ExecReadyExpr(state);
4278
4279	return state;
4280}
4281
4282/*
4283 * Build an ExprState that calls the given hash function(s) on the given
4284 * 'hash_exprs'.  When multiple expressions are present, the hash values
4285 * returned by each hash function are combined to produce a single hash value.
4286 *
4287 * desc: tuple descriptor for the to-be-hashed expressions
4288 * ops: TupleTableSlotOps for the TupleDesc
4289 * hashfunc_oids: Oid for each hash function to call, one for each 'hash_expr'
4290 * collations: collation to use when calling the hash function.
4291 * hash_expr: list of expressions to hash the value of
4292 * opstrict: array corresponding to the 'hashfunc_oids' to store op_strict()
4293 * parent: PlanState node that the 'hash_exprs' will be evaluated at
4294 * init_value: Normally 0, but can be set to other values to seed the hash
4295 * with some other value.  Using non-zero is slightly less efficient but can
4296 * be useful.
4297 * keep_nulls: if true, evaluation of the returned ExprState will abort early
4298 * returning NULL if the given hash function is strict and the Datum to hash
4299 * is null.  When set to false, any NULL input Datums are skipped.
4300 */
4301ExprState *
4302ExecBuildHash32Expr(TupleDesc desc, const TupleTableSlotOps *ops,
4303					const Oid *hashfunc_oids, const List *collations,
4304					const List *hash_exprs, const bool *opstrict,
4305					PlanState *parent, uint32 init_value, bool keep_nulls)
4306{
4307	ExprState  *state = makeNode(ExprState);
4308	ExprEvalStep scratch = {0};
4309	NullableDatum *iresult = NULL;
4310	List	   *adjust_jumps = NIL;
4311	ListCell   *lc;
4312	ListCell   *lc2;
4313	intptr_t	strict_opcode;
4314	intptr_t	opcode;
4315	int			num_exprs = list_length(hash_exprs);
4316
4317	Assert(num_exprs == list_length(collations));
4318
4319	state->parent = parent;
4320
4321	/* Insert setup steps as needed. */
4322	ExecCreateExprSetupSteps(state, (Node *) hash_exprs);
4323
4324	/*
4325	 * Make a place to store intermediate hash values between subsequent
4326	 * hashing of individual expressions.  We only need this if there is more
4327	 * than one expression to hash or an initial value plus one expression.
4328	 */
4329	if ((int64) num_exprs + (init_value != 0) > 1)
4330		iresult = palloc(sizeof(NullableDatum));
4331
4332	if (init_value == 0)
4333	{
4334		/*
4335		 * No initial value, so we can assign the result of the hash function
4336		 * for the first hash_expr without having to concern ourselves with
4337		 * combining the result with any initial value.
4338		 */
4339		strict_opcode = EEOP_HASHDATUM_FIRST_STRICT;
4340		opcode = EEOP_HASHDATUM_FIRST;
4341	}
4342	else
4343	{
4344		/*
4345		 * Set up operation to set the initial value.  Normally we store this
4346		 * in the intermediate hash value location, but if there are no exprs
4347		 * to hash, store it in the ExprState's result field.
4348		 */
4349		scratch.opcode = EEOP_HASHDATUM_SET_INITVAL;
4350		scratch.d.hashdatum_initvalue.init_value = UInt32GetDatum(init_value);
4351		scratch.resvalue = num_exprs > 0 ? &iresult->value : &state->resvalue;
4352		scratch.resnull = num_exprs > 0 ? &iresult->isnull : &state->resnull;
4353
4354		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4355
4356		/*
4357		 * When using an initial value use the NEXT32/NEXT32_STRICT ops as the
4358		 * FIRST/FIRST_STRICT ops would overwrite the stored initial value.
4359		 */
4360		strict_opcode = EEOP_HASHDATUM_NEXT32_STRICT;
4361		opcode = EEOP_HASHDATUM_NEXT32;
4362	}
4363
4364	forboth(lc, hash_exprs, lc2, collations)
4365	{
4366		Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
4367		FmgrInfo   *finfo;
4368		FunctionCallInfo fcinfo;
4369		int			i = foreach_current_index(lc);
4370		Oid			funcid;
4371		Oid			inputcollid = lfirst_oid(lc2);
4372
4373		funcid = hashfunc_oids[i];
4374
4375		/* Allocate hash function lookup data. */
4376		finfo = palloc0(sizeof(FmgrInfo));
4377		fcinfo = palloc0(SizeForFunctionCallInfo(1));
4378
4379		fmgr_info(funcid, finfo);
4380
4381		/*
4382		 * Build the steps to evaluate the hash function's argument have it so
4383		 * the value of that is stored in the 0th argument of the hash func.
4384		 */
4385		ExecInitExprRec(expr,
4386						state,
4387						&fcinfo->args[0].value,
4388						&fcinfo->args[0].isnull);
4389
4390		if (i == num_exprs - 1)
4391		{
4392			/* the result for hashing the final expr is stored in the state */
4393			scratch.resvalue = &state->resvalue;
4394			scratch.resnull = &state->resnull;
4395		}
4396		else
4397		{
4398			Assert(iresult != NULL);
4399
4400			/* intermediate values are stored in an intermediate result */
4401			scratch.resvalue = &iresult->value;
4402			scratch.resnull = &iresult->isnull;
4403		}
4404
4405		/*
4406		 * NEXT32 opcodes need to look at the intermediate result.  We might
4407		 * as well just set this for all ops.  FIRSTs won't look at it.
4408		 */
4409		scratch.d.hashdatum.iresult = iresult;
4410
4411		/* Initialize function call parameter structure too */
4412		InitFunctionCallInfoData(*fcinfo, finfo, 1, inputcollid, NULL, NULL);
4413
4414		scratch.d.hashdatum.finfo = finfo;
4415		scratch.d.hashdatum.fcinfo_data = fcinfo;
4416		scratch.d.hashdatum.fn_addr = finfo->fn_addr;
4417
4418		scratch.opcode = opstrict[i] && !keep_nulls ? strict_opcode : opcode;
4419		scratch.d.hashdatum.jumpdone = -1;
4420
4421		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4422		adjust_jumps = lappend_int(adjust_jumps, state->steps_len - 1);
4423
4424		/*
4425		 * For subsequent keys we must combine the hash value with the
4426		 * previous hashes.
4427		 */
4428		strict_opcode = EEOP_HASHDATUM_NEXT32_STRICT;
4429		opcode = EEOP_HASHDATUM_NEXT32;
4430	}
4431
4432	/* adjust jump targets */
4433	foreach(lc, adjust_jumps)
4434	{
4435		ExprEvalStep *as = &state->steps[lfirst_int(lc)];
4436
4437		Assert(as->opcode == EEOP_HASHDATUM_FIRST ||
4438			   as->opcode == EEOP_HASHDATUM_FIRST_STRICT ||
4439			   as->opcode == EEOP_HASHDATUM_NEXT32 ||
4440			   as->opcode == EEOP_HASHDATUM_NEXT32_STRICT);
4441		Assert(as->d.hashdatum.jumpdone == -1);
4442		as->d.hashdatum.jumpdone = state->steps_len;
4443	}
4444
4445	scratch.resvalue = NULL;
4446	scratch.resnull = NULL;
4447	scratch.opcode = EEOP_DONE_RETURN;
4448	ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4449
4450	ExecReadyExpr(state);
4451
4452	return state;
4453}
4454
4455/*
4456 * Build equality expression that can be evaluated using ExecQual(), returning
4457 * true if the expression context's inner/outer tuple are NOT DISTINCT. I.e
4458 * two nulls match, a null and a not-null don't match.
4459 *
4460 * desc: tuple descriptor of the to-be-compared tuples
4461 * numCols: the number of attributes to be examined
4462 * keyColIdx: array of attribute column numbers
4463 * eqFunctions: array of function oids of the equality functions to use
4464 * parent: parent executor node
4465 */
4466ExprState *
4467ExecBuildGroupingEqual(TupleDesc ldesc, TupleDesc rdesc,
4468					   const TupleTableSlotOps *lops, const TupleTableSlotOps *rops,
4469					   int numCols,
4470					   const AttrNumber *keyColIdx,
4471					   const Oid *eqfunctions,
4472					   const Oid *collations,
4473					   PlanState *parent)
4474{
4475	ExprState  *state = makeNode(ExprState);
4476	ExprEvalStep scratch = {0};
4477	int			maxatt = -1;
4478	List	   *adjust_jumps = NIL;
4479	ListCell   *lc;
4480
4481	/*
4482	 * When no columns are actually compared, the result's always true. See
4483	 * special case in ExecQual().
4484	 */
4485	if (numCols == 0)
4486		return NULL;
4487
4488	state->expr = NULL;
4489	state->flags = EEO_FLAG_IS_QUAL;
4490	state->parent = parent;
4491
4492	scratch.resvalue = &state->resvalue;
4493	scratch.resnull = &state->resnull;
4494
4495	/* compute max needed attribute */
4496	for (int natt = 0; natt < numCols; natt++)
4497	{
4498		int			attno = keyColIdx[natt];
4499
4500		if (attno > maxatt)
4501			maxatt = attno;
4502	}
4503	Assert(maxatt >= 0);
4504
4505	/* push deform steps */
4506	scratch.opcode = EEOP_INNER_FETCHSOME;
4507	scratch.d.fetch.last_var = maxatt;
4508	scratch.d.fetch.fixed = false;
4509	scratch.d.fetch.known_desc = ldesc;
4510	scratch.d.fetch.kind = lops;
4511	if (ExecComputeSlotInfo(state, &scratch))
4512		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4513
4514	scratch.opcode = EEOP_OUTER_FETCHSOME;
4515	scratch.d.fetch.last_var = maxatt;
4516	scratch.d.fetch.fixed = false;
4517	scratch.d.fetch.known_desc = rdesc;
4518	scratch.d.fetch.kind = rops;
4519	if (ExecComputeSlotInfo(state, &scratch))
4520		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4521
4522	/*
4523	 * Start comparing at the last field (least significant sort key). That's
4524	 * the most likely to be different if we are dealing with sorted input.
4525	 */
4526	for (int natt = numCols; --natt >= 0;)
4527	{
4528		int			attno = keyColIdx[natt];
4529		Form_pg_attribute latt = TupleDescAttr(ldesc, attno - 1);
4530		Form_pg_attribute ratt = TupleDescAttr(rdesc, attno - 1);
4531		Oid			foid = eqfunctions[natt];
4532		Oid			collid = collations[natt];
4533		FmgrInfo   *finfo;
4534		FunctionCallInfo fcinfo;
4535		AclResult	aclresult;
4536
4537		/* Check permission to call function */
4538		aclresult = object_aclcheck(ProcedureRelationId, foid, GetUserId(), ACL_EXECUTE);
4539		if (aclresult != ACLCHECK_OK)
4540			aclcheck_error(aclresult, OBJECT_FUNCTION, get_func_name(foid));
4541
4542		InvokeFunctionExecuteHook(foid);
4543
4544		/* Set up the primary fmgr lookup information */
4545		finfo = palloc0(sizeof(FmgrInfo));
4546		fcinfo = palloc0(SizeForFunctionCallInfo(2));
4547		fmgr_info(foid, finfo);
4548		fmgr_info_set_expr(NULL, finfo);
4549		InitFunctionCallInfoData(*fcinfo, finfo, 2,
4550								 collid, NULL, NULL);
4551
4552		/* left arg */
4553		scratch.opcode = EEOP_INNER_VAR;
4554		scratch.d.var.attnum = attno - 1;
4555		scratch.d.var.vartype = latt->atttypid;
4556		scratch.d.var.varreturningtype = VAR_RETURNING_DEFAULT;
4557		scratch.resvalue = &fcinfo->args[0].value;
4558		scratch.resnull = &fcinfo->args[0].isnull;
4559		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4560
4561		/* right arg */
4562		scratch.opcode = EEOP_OUTER_VAR;
4563		scratch.d.var.attnum = attno - 1;
4564		scratch.d.var.vartype = ratt->atttypid;
4565		scratch.d.var.varreturningtype = VAR_RETURNING_DEFAULT;
4566		scratch.resvalue = &fcinfo->args[1].value;
4567		scratch.resnull = &fcinfo->args[1].isnull;
4568		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4569
4570		/* evaluate distinctness */
4571		scratch.opcode = EEOP_NOT_DISTINCT;
4572		scratch.d.func.finfo = finfo;
4573		scratch.d.func.fcinfo_data = fcinfo;
4574		scratch.d.func.fn_addr = finfo->fn_addr;
4575		scratch.d.func.nargs = 2;
4576		scratch.resvalue = &state->resvalue;
4577		scratch.resnull = &state->resnull;
4578		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4579
4580		/* then emit EEOP_QUAL to detect if result is false (or null) */
4581		scratch.opcode = EEOP_QUAL;
4582		scratch.d.qualexpr.jumpdone = -1;
4583		scratch.resvalue = &state->resvalue;
4584		scratch.resnull = &state->resnull;
4585		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4586		adjust_jumps = lappend_int(adjust_jumps,
4587								   state->steps_len - 1);
4588	}
4589
4590	/* adjust jump targets */
4591	foreach(lc, adjust_jumps)
4592	{
4593		ExprEvalStep *as = &state->steps[lfirst_int(lc)];
4594
4595		Assert(as->opcode == EEOP_QUAL);
4596		Assert(as->d.qualexpr.jumpdone == -1);
4597		as->d.qualexpr.jumpdone = state->steps_len;
4598	}
4599
4600	scratch.resvalue = NULL;
4601	scratch.resnull = NULL;
4602	scratch.opcode = EEOP_DONE_RETURN;
4603	ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4604
4605	ExecReadyExpr(state);
4606
4607	return state;
4608}
4609
4610/*
4611 * Build equality expression that can be evaluated using ExecQual(), returning
4612 * true if the expression context's inner/outer tuples are equal.  Datums in
4613 * the inner/outer slots are assumed to be in the same order and quantity as
4614 * the 'eqfunctions' parameter.  NULLs are treated as equal.
4615 *
4616 * desc: tuple descriptor of the to-be-compared tuples
4617 * lops: the slot ops for the inner tuple slots
4618 * rops: the slot ops for the outer tuple slots
4619 * eqFunctions: array of function oids of the equality functions to use
4620 * this must be the same length as the 'param_exprs' list.
4621 * collations: collation Oids to use for equality comparison. Must be the
4622 * same length as the 'param_exprs' list.
4623 * parent: parent executor node
4624 */
4625ExprState *
4626ExecBuildParamSetEqual(TupleDesc desc,
4627					   const TupleTableSlotOps *lops,
4628					   const TupleTableSlotOps *rops,
4629					   const Oid *eqfunctions,
4630					   const Oid *collations,
4631					   const List *param_exprs,
4632					   PlanState *parent)
4633{
4634	ExprState  *state = makeNode(ExprState);
4635	ExprEvalStep scratch = {0};
4636	int			maxatt = list_length(param_exprs);
4637	List	   *adjust_jumps = NIL;
4638	ListCell   *lc;
4639
4640	state->expr = NULL;
4641	state->flags = EEO_FLAG_IS_QUAL;
4642	state->parent = parent;
4643
4644	scratch.resvalue = &state->resvalue;
4645	scratch.resnull = &state->resnull;
4646
4647	/* push deform steps */
4648	scratch.opcode = EEOP_INNER_FETCHSOME;
4649	scratch.d.fetch.last_var = maxatt;
4650	scratch.d.fetch.fixed = false;
4651	scratch.d.fetch.known_desc = desc;
4652	scratch.d.fetch.kind = lops;
4653	if (ExecComputeSlotInfo(state, &scratch))
4654		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4655
4656	scratch.opcode = EEOP_OUTER_FETCHSOME;
4657	scratch.d.fetch.last_var = maxatt;
4658	scratch.d.fetch.fixed = false;
4659	scratch.d.fetch.known_desc = desc;
4660	scratch.d.fetch.kind = rops;
4661	if (ExecComputeSlotInfo(state, &scratch))
4662		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4663
4664	for (int attno = 0; attno < maxatt; attno++)
4665	{
4666		Form_pg_attribute att = TupleDescAttr(desc, attno);
4667		Oid			foid = eqfunctions[attno];
4668		Oid			collid = collations[attno];
4669		FmgrInfo   *finfo;
4670		FunctionCallInfo fcinfo;
4671		AclResult	aclresult;
4672
4673		/* Check permission to call function */
4674		aclresult = object_aclcheck(ProcedureRelationId, foid, GetUserId(), ACL_EXECUTE);
4675		if (aclresult != ACLCHECK_OK)
4676			aclcheck_error(aclresult, OBJECT_FUNCTION, get_func_name(foid));
4677
4678		InvokeFunctionExecuteHook(foid);
4679
4680		/* Set up the primary fmgr lookup information */
4681		finfo = palloc0(sizeof(FmgrInfo));
4682		fcinfo = palloc0(SizeForFunctionCallInfo(2));
4683		fmgr_info(foid, finfo);
4684		fmgr_info_set_expr(NULL, finfo);
4685		InitFunctionCallInfoData(*fcinfo, finfo, 2,
4686								 collid, NULL, NULL);
4687
4688		/* left arg */
4689		scratch.opcode = EEOP_INNER_VAR;
4690		scratch.d.var.attnum = attno;
4691		scratch.d.var.vartype = att->atttypid;
