4692		scratch.d.var.varreturningtype = VAR_RETURNING_DEFAULT;
4693		scratch.resvalue = &fcinfo->args[0].value;
4694		scratch.resnull = &fcinfo->args[0].isnull;
4695		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4696
4697		/* right arg */
4698		scratch.opcode = EEOP_OUTER_VAR;
4699		scratch.d.var.attnum = attno;
4700		scratch.d.var.vartype = att->atttypid;
4701		scratch.d.var.varreturningtype = VAR_RETURNING_DEFAULT;
4702		scratch.resvalue = &fcinfo->args[1].value;
4703		scratch.resnull = &fcinfo->args[1].isnull;
4704		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4705
4706		/* evaluate distinctness */
4707		scratch.opcode = EEOP_NOT_DISTINCT;
4708		scratch.d.func.finfo = finfo;
4709		scratch.d.func.fcinfo_data = fcinfo;
4710		scratch.d.func.fn_addr = finfo->fn_addr;
4711		scratch.d.func.nargs = 2;
4712		scratch.resvalue = &state->resvalue;
4713		scratch.resnull = &state->resnull;
4714		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4715
4716		/* then emit EEOP_QUAL to detect if result is false (or null) */
4717		scratch.opcode = EEOP_QUAL;
4718		scratch.d.qualexpr.jumpdone = -1;
4719		scratch.resvalue = &state->resvalue;
4720		scratch.resnull = &state->resnull;
4721		ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4722		adjust_jumps = lappend_int(adjust_jumps,
4723								   state->steps_len - 1);
4724	}
4725
4726	/* adjust jump targets */
4727	foreach(lc, adjust_jumps)
4728	{
4729		ExprEvalStep *as = &state->steps[lfirst_int(lc)];
4730
4731		Assert(as->opcode == EEOP_QUAL);
4732		Assert(as->d.qualexpr.jumpdone == -1);
4733		as->d.qualexpr.jumpdone = state->steps_len;
4734	}
4735
4736	scratch.resvalue = NULL;
4737	scratch.resnull = NULL;
4738	scratch.opcode = EEOP_DONE_RETURN;
4739	ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
4740
4741	ExecReadyExpr(state);
4742
4743	return state;
4744}
4745
4746/*
4747 * Push steps to evaluate a JsonExpr and its various subsidiary expressions.
4748 */
4749static void
4750ExecInitJsonExpr(JsonExpr *jsexpr, ExprState *state,
4751				 Datum *resv, bool *resnull,
4752				 ExprEvalStep *scratch)
4753{
4754	JsonExprState *jsestate = palloc0(sizeof(JsonExprState));
4755	ListCell   *argexprlc;
4756	ListCell   *argnamelc;
4757	List	   *jumps_return_null = NIL;
4758	List	   *jumps_to_end = NIL;
4759	ListCell   *lc;
4760	ErrorSaveContext *escontext;
4761	bool		returning_domain =
4762		get_typtype(jsexpr->returning->typid) == TYPTYPE_DOMAIN;
4763
4764	Assert(jsexpr->on_error != NULL);
4765
4766	jsestate->jsexpr = jsexpr;
4767
4768	/*
4769	 * Evaluate formatted_expr storing the result into
4770	 * jsestate->formatted_expr.
4771	 */
4772	ExecInitExprRec((Expr *) jsexpr->formatted_expr, state,
4773					&jsestate->formatted_expr.value,
4774					&jsestate->formatted_expr.isnull);
4775
4776	/* JUMP to return NULL if formatted_expr evaluates to NULL */
4777	jumps_return_null = lappend_int(jumps_return_null, state->steps_len);
4778	scratch->opcode = EEOP_JUMP_IF_NULL;
4779	scratch->resnull = &jsestate->formatted_expr.isnull;
4780	scratch->d.jump.jumpdone = -1;	/* set below */
4781	ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
4782
4783	/*
4784	 * Evaluate pathspec expression storing the result into
4785	 * jsestate->pathspec.
4786	 */
4787	ExecInitExprRec((Expr *) jsexpr->path_spec, state,
4788					&jsestate->pathspec.value,
4789					&jsestate->pathspec.isnull);
4790
4791	/* JUMP to return NULL if path_spec evaluates to NULL */
4792	jumps_return_null = lappend_int(jumps_return_null, state->steps_len);
4793	scratch->opcode = EEOP_JUMP_IF_NULL;
4794	scratch->resnull = &jsestate->pathspec.isnull;
4795	scratch->d.jump.jumpdone = -1;	/* set below */
4796	ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
4797
4798	/* Steps to compute PASSING args. */
4799	jsestate->args = NIL;
4800	forboth(argexprlc, jsexpr->passing_values,
4801			argnamelc, jsexpr->passing_names)
4802	{
4803		Expr	   *argexpr = (Expr *) lfirst(argexprlc);
4804		String	   *argname = lfirst_node(String, argnamelc);
4805		JsonPathVariable *var = palloc(sizeof(*var));
4806
4807		var->name = argname->sval;
4808		var->namelen = strlen(var->name);
4809		var->typid = exprType((Node *) argexpr);
4810		var->typmod = exprTypmod((Node *) argexpr);
4811
4812		ExecInitExprRec((Expr *) argexpr, state, &var->value, &var->isnull);
4813
4814		jsestate->args = lappend(jsestate->args, var);
4815	}
4816
4817	/* Step for jsonpath evaluation; see ExecEvalJsonExprPath(). */
4818	scratch->opcode = EEOP_JSONEXPR_PATH;
4819	scratch->resvalue = resv;
4820	scratch->resnull = resnull;
4821	scratch->d.jsonexpr.jsestate = jsestate;
4822	ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
4823
4824	/*
4825	 * Step to return NULL after jumping to skip the EEOP_JSONEXPR_PATH step
4826	 * when either formatted_expr or pathspec is NULL.  Adjust jump target
4827	 * addresses of JUMPs that we added above.
4828	 */
4829	foreach(lc, jumps_return_null)
4830	{
4831		ExprEvalStep *as = &state->steps[lfirst_int(lc)];
4832
4833		as->d.jump.jumpdone = state->steps_len;
4834	}
4835	scratch->opcode = EEOP_CONST;
4836	scratch->resvalue = resv;
4837	scratch->resnull = resnull;
4838	scratch->d.constval.value = (Datum) 0;
4839	scratch->d.constval.isnull = true;
4840	ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
4841
4842	escontext = jsexpr->on_error->btype != JSON_BEHAVIOR_ERROR ?
4843		&jsestate->escontext : NULL;
4844
4845	/*
4846	 * To handle coercion errors softly, use the following ErrorSaveContext to
4847	 * pass to ExecInitExprRec() when initializing the coercion expressions
4848	 * and in the EEOP_JSONEXPR_COERCION step.
4849	 */
4850	jsestate->escontext.type = T_ErrorSaveContext;
4851
4852	/*
4853	 * Steps to coerce the result value computed by EEOP_JSONEXPR_PATH or the
4854	 * NULL returned on NULL input as described above.
4855	 */
4856	jsestate->jump_eval_coercion = -1;
4857	if (jsexpr->use_json_coercion)
4858	{
4859		jsestate->jump_eval_coercion = state->steps_len;
4860
4861		ExecInitJsonCoercion(state, jsexpr->returning, escontext,
4862							 jsexpr->omit_quotes,
4863							 jsexpr->op == JSON_EXISTS_OP,
4864							 resv, resnull);
4865	}
4866	else if (jsexpr->use_io_coercion)
4867	{
4868		/*
4869		 * Here we only need to initialize the FunctionCallInfo for the target
4870		 * type's input function, which is called by ExecEvalJsonExprPath()
4871		 * itself, so no additional step is necessary.
4872		 */
4873		Oid			typinput;
4874		Oid			typioparam;
4875		FmgrInfo   *finfo;
4876		FunctionCallInfo fcinfo;
4877
4878		getTypeInputInfo(jsexpr->returning->typid, &typinput, &typioparam);
4879		finfo = palloc0(sizeof(FmgrInfo));
4880		fcinfo = palloc0(SizeForFunctionCallInfo(3));
4881		fmgr_info(typinput, finfo);
4882		fmgr_info_set_expr((Node *) jsexpr->returning, finfo);
4883		InitFunctionCallInfoData(*fcinfo, finfo, 3, InvalidOid, NULL, NULL);
4884
4885		/*
4886		 * We can preload the second and third arguments for the input
4887		 * function, since they're constants.
4888		 */
4889		fcinfo->args[1].value = ObjectIdGetDatum(typioparam);
4890		fcinfo->args[1].isnull = false;
4891		fcinfo->args[2].value = Int32GetDatum(jsexpr->returning->typmod);
4892		fcinfo->args[2].isnull = false;
4893		fcinfo->context = (Node *) escontext;
4894
4895		jsestate->input_fcinfo = fcinfo;
4896	}
4897
4898	/*
4899	 * Add a special step, if needed, to check if the coercion evaluation ran
4900	 * into an error but was not thrown because the ON ERROR behavior is not
4901	 * ERROR.  It will set jsestate->error if an error did occur.
4902	 */
4903	if (jsestate->jump_eval_coercion >= 0 && escontext != NULL)
4904	{
4905		scratch->opcode = EEOP_JSONEXPR_COERCION_FINISH;
4906		scratch->d.jsonexpr.jsestate = jsestate;
4907		ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
4908	}
4909
4910	jsestate->jump_empty = jsestate->jump_error = -1;
4911
4912	/*
4913	 * Step to check jsestate->error and return the ON ERROR expression if
4914	 * there is one.  This handles both the errors that occur during jsonpath
4915	 * evaluation in EEOP_JSONEXPR_PATH and subsequent coercion evaluation.
4916	 *
4917	 * Speed up common cases by avoiding extra steps for a NULL-valued ON
4918	 * ERROR expression unless RETURNING a domain type, where constraints must
4919	 * be checked. ExecEvalJsonExprPath() already returns NULL on error,
4920	 * making additional steps unnecessary in typical scenarios. Note that the
4921	 * default ON ERROR behavior for JSON_VALUE() and JSON_QUERY() is to
4922	 * return NULL.
4923	 */
4924	if (jsexpr->on_error->btype != JSON_BEHAVIOR_ERROR &&
4925		(!(IsA(jsexpr->on_error->expr, Const) &&
4926		   ((Const *) jsexpr->on_error->expr)->constisnull) ||
4927		 returning_domain))
4928	{
4929		ErrorSaveContext *saved_escontext;
4930
4931		jsestate->jump_error = state->steps_len;
4932
4933		/* JUMP to end if false, that is, skip the ON ERROR expression. */
4934		jumps_to_end = lappend_int(jumps_to_end, state->steps_len);
4935		scratch->opcode = EEOP_JUMP_IF_NOT_TRUE;
4936		scratch->resvalue = &jsestate->error.value;
4937		scratch->resnull = &jsestate->error.isnull;
4938		scratch->d.jump.jumpdone = -1;	/* set below */
4939		ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
4940
4941		/*
4942		 * Steps to evaluate the ON ERROR expression; handle errors softly to
4943		 * rethrow them in COERCION_FINISH step that will be added later.
4944		 */
4945		saved_escontext = state->escontext;
4946		state->escontext = escontext;
4947		ExecInitExprRec((Expr *) jsexpr->on_error->expr,
4948						state, resv, resnull);
4949		state->escontext = saved_escontext;
4950
4951		/* Step to coerce the ON ERROR expression if needed */
4952		if (jsexpr->on_error->coerce)
4953			ExecInitJsonCoercion(state, jsexpr->returning, escontext,
4954								 jsexpr->omit_quotes, false,
4955								 resv, resnull);
4956
4957		/*
4958		 * Add a COERCION_FINISH step to check for errors that may occur when
4959		 * coercing and rethrow them.
4960		 */
4961		if (jsexpr->on_error->coerce ||
4962			IsA(jsexpr->on_error->expr, CoerceViaIO) ||
4963			IsA(jsexpr->on_error->expr, CoerceToDomain))
4964		{
4965			scratch->opcode = EEOP_JSONEXPR_COERCION_FINISH;
4966			scratch->resvalue = resv;
4967			scratch->resnull = resnull;
4968			scratch->d.jsonexpr.jsestate = jsestate;
4969			ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
4970		}
4971
4972		/* JUMP to end to skip the ON EMPTY steps added below. */
4973		jumps_to_end = lappend_int(jumps_to_end, state->steps_len);
4974		scratch->opcode = EEOP_JUMP;
4975		scratch->d.jump.jumpdone = -1;
4976		ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
4977	}
4978
4979	/*
4980	 * Step to check jsestate->empty and return the ON EMPTY expression if
4981	 * there is one.
4982	 *
4983	 * See the comment above for details on the optimization for NULL-valued
4984	 * expressions.
4985	 */
4986	if (jsexpr->on_empty != NULL &&
4987		jsexpr->on_empty->btype != JSON_BEHAVIOR_ERROR &&
4988		(!(IsA(jsexpr->on_empty->expr, Const) &&
4989		   ((Const *) jsexpr->on_empty->expr)->constisnull) ||
4990		 returning_domain))
4991	{
4992		ErrorSaveContext *saved_escontext;
4993
4994		jsestate->jump_empty = state->steps_len;
4995
4996		/* JUMP to end if false, that is, skip the ON EMPTY expression. */
4997		jumps_to_end = lappend_int(jumps_to_end, state->steps_len);
4998		scratch->opcode = EEOP_JUMP_IF_NOT_TRUE;
4999		scratch->resvalue = &jsestate->empty.value;
5000		scratch->resnull = &jsestate->empty.isnull;
5001		scratch->d.jump.jumpdone = -1;	/* set below */
5002		ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
5003
5004		/*
5005		 * Steps to evaluate the ON EMPTY expression; handle errors softly to
5006		 * rethrow them in COERCION_FINISH step that will be added later.
5007		 */
5008		saved_escontext = state->escontext;
5009		state->escontext = escontext;
5010		ExecInitExprRec((Expr *) jsexpr->on_empty->expr,
5011						state, resv, resnull);
5012		state->escontext = saved_escontext;
5013
5014		/* Step to coerce the ON EMPTY expression if needed */
5015		if (jsexpr->on_empty->coerce)
5016			ExecInitJsonCoercion(state, jsexpr->returning, escontext,
5017								 jsexpr->omit_quotes, false,
5018								 resv, resnull);
5019
5020		/*
5021		 * Add a COERCION_FINISH step to check for errors that may occur when
5022		 * coercing and rethrow them.
5023		 */
5024		if (jsexpr->on_empty->coerce ||
5025			IsA(jsexpr->on_empty->expr, CoerceViaIO) ||
5026			IsA(jsexpr->on_empty->expr, CoerceToDomain))
5027		{
5028
5029			scratch->opcode = EEOP_JSONEXPR_COERCION_FINISH;
5030			scratch->resvalue = resv;
5031			scratch->resnull = resnull;
5032			scratch->d.jsonexpr.jsestate = jsestate;
5033			ExprEvalPushStep(state, scratch);
5034		}
5035	}
5036
5037	foreach(lc, jumps_to_end)
5038	{
5039		ExprEvalStep *as = &state->steps[lfirst_int(lc)];
5040
5041		as->d.jump.jumpdone = state->steps_len;
5042	}
5043
5044	jsestate->jump_end = state->steps_len;
5045}
5046
5047/*
5048 * Initialize a EEOP_JSONEXPR_COERCION step to coerce the value given in resv
5049 * to the given RETURNING type.
5050 */
5051static void
5052ExecInitJsonCoercion(ExprState *state, JsonReturning *returning,
5053					 ErrorSaveContext *escontext, bool omit_quotes,
5054					 bool exists_coerce,
5055					 Datum *resv, bool *resnull)
5056{
5057	ExprEvalStep scratch = {0};
5058
5059	/* For json_populate_type() */
5060	scratch.opcode = EEOP_JSONEXPR_COERCION;
5061	scratch.resvalue = resv;
5062	scratch.resnull = resnull;
5063	scratch.d.jsonexpr_coercion.targettype = returning->typid;
5064	scratch.d.jsonexpr_coercion.targettypmod = returning->typmod;
5065	scratch.d.jsonexpr_coercion.json_coercion_cache = NULL;
5066	scratch.d.jsonexpr_coercion.escontext = escontext;
5067	scratch.d.jsonexpr_coercion.omit_quotes = omit_quotes;
5068	scratch.d.jsonexpr_coercion.exists_coerce = exists_coerce;
5069	scratch.d.jsonexpr_coercion.exists_cast_to_int = exists_coerce &&
5070		getBaseType(returning->typid) == INT4OID;
5071	scratch.d.jsonexpr_coercion.exists_check_domain = exists_coerce &&
5072		DomainHasConstraints(returning->typid);
5073	ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
5074}
